programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The shooting of a petty officer looks like an open-and-shut case when a Russian man is found with a recently fired gun in his car. However, the investigation takes a dramatic turn when it is revealed he is working as a spy for the Department of Homeland Security. Military crime drama, with Mark Harmon, Cote de Pablo and David McCallum

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Eric pulls the team together when he finds a video of kidnapped agent Dominic Vail posted on a jihadist website, with the terrorists holding him stating that he will be executed within 24 hours unless a comrade of theirs being held by the US government is released. One of the captors is identified as a being an associate of Sam, and his involvement also seems to indicate Dom is being held in Sudan. Crime drama, starring LL Cool J

programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Law & Order
programme.desc: Captain Cragen becomes the subject of an internal investigation into evidence tampering and the laundering of drug money - leaving Logan and Greevey struggling to clear his name

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team uncovers a fraudulent charity that preys on those searching for missing military personnel while investigating the murder of a retired Master Sergeant. Meanwhile, Bishop agonises over the results of her probation evaluation, and Tony contemplates adopting a stray cat that followed him home. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy captain Jake Marsden is murdered in his hotel room and inquiries lead to marine lieutenant Emma Reynolds - who tells the team she was the intended target. After being stationed in Kabul, she became pregnant by a local man called Amir who was later killed in action - and Abby discovers he was the sole male heir of a wealthy family that owns a large tract of land in a politically sensitive part of Afghanistan

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: An attack on a private plane immediately after it lands turns out to be the hijack of the secret army transfer of terrorist leader Ala A Din Keshwar, the man whose release was demanded in exchange for Dom's life. Callen and the rest of the NCIS team vow to get their hands on Keshwar before he can flee the country. Meanwhile, Hetty offers her resignation to Vance following Dom's death

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the cause of a hit-and-run incident in which a Petty Officer has been killed. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part one of two. Walker helps a man come to terms with his past in order to carry on his father's legacy. Wilma Knight makes a cameo appearance alongside son Chuck Norris

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a Navy commander last seen in the company of a mysterious blonde woman, who turns out to be missing NCIS agent and Tony's ex-lover EJ Barrett. She identifies the murderer as Seal-turned-assassin Marcellus Dane, who is suspected of working for Casey Stratton - a member of black ops unit Phantom Eight. Guest starring Sarah Jane Morris

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Callen's cover is blown while he is working undercover and he sends a coded text to Hetty, who effectively cuts him off from NCIS as she makes it the team's top priority to discover who found out his real identity and how. A man called Eugene Keelson contacts Callen and threatens to reveal information about his past unless he runs an errand for him, which involves delivering a flash drive to Bulgarian gang. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An enigmatic killer shoots dead a businessman spending Thanksgiving at a hotel in the city, then takes his glasses, jewellery and ID to Washington Dulles airport, which has been brought to a stand-still by a massive snowstorm. As the murderer's actions take effect, a terror alert is issued, leaving all northern US airports in a state of panic, and leaving it up to DiNozzo, Bishop and her husband - all of whom are trapped at Dulles by the weather - to bring the armed villain to justice. Crime drama, starring Michael Weatherley

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An estate agent and two clients discover the body of Lt Com Maya Burris, a naval attache who had been working in Egypt, and her detective husband Nick arrives on the scene to demand answers. Using documents discovered on the victim's boat, Abby links her to Barry Norton, who is suspected of having sold government secrets to a Middle Eastern terrorist group known as the SRC

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Keelson is found to have had a huge file labelled 'Callen, G', but it is destroyed remotely before Callen can retrieve it. The team is assigned to monitor Iranian terrorists trying to get their hands on a huge hidden fortune that belonged to the former shah, and the case appears to have a concrete link to Callen when a woman in the group's ranks is identified as being his sister Amy. Starring Chris O'Donnell

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: McGee deals with his father's recent death by writing him a letter outlining the details of his latest investigation. The case involved tracking down a hactivist collective known as Krampus, who were thought to have been responsible for an incident in which the internet crashed all over Washington, DC. McGee and Abby brought in three known cyber-terrorists for questioning, and discover evidence that links the trio to an attempted breach of the Congress e-mail server and archive. However, when this line of inquiry reaches a dead-end, the team picks up a new lead that suggests an isolated hacker from Wisconsin may be responsible. This 'lone wolf' scenario does not seem plausible to McGee, though, as he recalls a lesson his late father taught him. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paige's son turns up for an eventful weekend, which starts in the police station and ends in protest. As a result he has trouble finding the right moment to tell his father he wants to quit his medical studies. It isn't until they are drawn together in the fight to save a friend's livelihood that they can talk like father and son. Drama, starring Nigel Havers, Hugh Dancy and Jane Gurnett

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Chris comes up with a surprise, while Sharon's contentment is shattered by the medical advice all women dread - she has got to lose weight

programme.desc: Tom sets out to prove himself by learning to drive and Harvey again applies for membership of the golf club, masquerading as a clergyman. Sitcom set in the Bayview Retirement Village, starring Stephanie Cole and Graham Crowden

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Alfonse is whisked to hospital with heart trouble after being interrogated by the Gestapo. Rene feigns a bout of food poisoning in a bid to rescue his friend before he spills the beans about the stolen money. With Gorden Kaye, Kenneth Connor and Carmen Silvera

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Howard secretly buys a van and asks Wesley to customise it for long journeys, but Compo insists on pushing the vehicle to its limits. Truly thinks Clegg is paying too high a tax, so he drags his friend to a council meeting. Auntie Wainwright has Smiler deliver a truck load of junk that the recipient did not order. Peter Sallis and Howard Fyfe star

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: The coffin of local poet Declan O'Grady falls from the hearse revealing that the corpse inside is not his but a young woman identifiable only by a notable scarlet tattoo. Funeral director Warren Bugle directs Mike, Kristin and Breen to Wadsworth Manor where a mysterious role-playing game was conducted the night before Declan's death.

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: Brian finds the library card of cold case victim Dr Richard Symes, leading the team to reinvestigate the death of the professor, who perished after falling from the roof of his college three years' previously. Sandra and Gerry interview Symes' widow, who believes principal Jeremy Ventham drove her husband to suicide following a conflict over teaching methods. Crime drama, starring Alun Armstrong and Amanda Redman

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: A severed finger is brought to the Lyell Centre by DCI Rory Goodchild and immigration enforcement officer Pia Butler, who ask Nikki and Thomas to test for a DNA match with 17 year-old Syrian illegal immigrant Akka Khoury. The teenager was sent the digit by a people-smuggler who claims to have her mother and is demanding money for her safe return. Meanwhile, Akka's 12 year-old cousin Sosa is trying to enter the UK illegally hidden in a van on a cross-channel ferry. Emilia Fox stars

Neil takes an early-morning call from James Tennant, who believes he has found a new lead in the search for his missing daughter, but the visions of the psychic hired by the despairing father merely add to the mist of confusion surrounding the case

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Steve makes a serious error of judgement, Pat gets an offer she can't refuse, and Natalie develops an interest in car maintenance

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Della won't tell Binnie where she spent the night. Steve wants out of the salon. David wants Pat to lunch with Roy Evans in order to do a car deal

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: New recruit Craig Ross arrives at Blackwall and Mooney begins his bid for leadership of the brigade, only to put his life at risk in the process

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: A woman who agrees to be a surrogate mother for her sister finds her decision has far-reaching consequences. Medical drama, starring Gary Mavers and Fiona Gillies

programme.title: Dangerfield
programme.desc: Paige responds to a call for help from a boy whose father has been gripped by depression since the death of his wife. Meanwhile, a chance meeting between the surgeon's lover and police colleague DI Cramer creates a frosty atmosphere. Drama, starring Nigel Havers and Jane Gurnett

programme.title: Birds of a Feather
programme.desc: Sharon and Tracey think supernatural forces could be at work when the video recorder begins to act rather strangely. Comedy, starring Pauline Quirke, Linda Robson and Lesley Joseph

programme.title: Waiting for God
programme.desc: Jane and Diana's strip-club hen night proves to be a real eye-opener, then a double booking with a funeral at the church throws the happy couples' wedding plans into chaos. Comedy, starring Graham Crowden and Stephanie Cole

programme.title: 'Allo 'Allo!
programme.desc: Herr Flick arrests Leclerc and issues a proclamation he'll be shot if the stolen Gestapo money is not returned, while Edith is in a romantic mood and Fanny decides to bake a potato

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: On Truly's advice, Compo parades a more daring and glamorous image to impress Nora - and her reaction transports him to new heights. Rural comedy, starring Frank Thornton and Bill Owen

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries
programme.desc: Shepherd and his team are confronted with the sight of a Santa Claus with a slit throat. The victim is actually much-loved local mayor Evan Whitestone, who had dressed as Santa every year for the Christmas parade. Suspicion soon turns to the four rivals for his mayoral chains in a forthcoming election. Neill Rea and Fern Sutherland star

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: A recent rape is linked to two similar attacks at a chocolate factory 10 years previously, leaving the team with no choice but to reopen all three cases. The detectives uncover news not only of an unhappy workforce, but also of an alleged suicide, and their investigation becomes even more complicated when they discover a severed finger was found in one of the company's snacks. After a visit to the doctor, Gerry decides to quit smoking, and instead of resorting to nicotine patches or gum, he enlists Sandra to help him quit

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The body of 20 year-old Lorna Katz is discovered drifting in the River Lea, with evidence suggesting she was tied up somewhere before she floated downstream. However, the death takes on an ever-more sinister significance when the team discovers that Lorna was the best friend of Ruth Tresize, a teenager who disappeared three years previously, with the missing girl's mother Ali becoming fixated on Nikki for information, convinced that the two incidents are connected

programme.title: Y Cosmos
programme.desc: Documentary revealing how the existence of Jupiter is imperative to the development of every living thing in the solar system

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Cheetahs and endangered spiders both enter mating season. A Bactrian camel undergoes laser treatment, and a party is thrown to celebrate the ninth birthday of AJ the red panda

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a property in Manchester and has some motorised fun with a toy car at a house in Liverpool. Plus, the team revisits a London project Lucy Alexander first saw three years previously. It didn't have a roof back then, but it's been worth waiting for the finish

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: The A&E staff have to juggle space when a patient arrives with mystery abdominal pains

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles starts this programme at the British Bird of Prey centre in the National Botanic Garden of Wales and asks the people of Carmarthenshire to help identify a mystery object. The red and blue teams then go shopping with experts Ochuko Ojiri and Jonathan Pratt, hoping to find items of value to take to auction in Cardiff

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Ryan has a problem with three little words, and Ash shares his fears with Sully

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team of names from the world of food and drink takes on the general knowledge champions, as Oz Clarke, Ed Baines, Paul Rankin, Jonathan Phang and Reza Mahammad hope to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a property-searching mission in North Devon with a house buyer who has been tasked by her husband to find their perfect country home on a £600,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and the Rich brothers create a garden in Thatcham, Berkshire, for widower Lee, who wants a space where his children can have fun and play, but also remember their mum

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton embark on the second leg of their five-part expedition, heading to Worcestershire, Warwickshire and the West Midlands

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Amol Rajan and Alex Jones

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Natalie Cassidy and Shona McGarty join Stacey Dooley to talk about Sonia Fowler and Whitney Dean, reliving classic scenes and revealing juicy behind-the-scenes gossip

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Part two of two. Michael Mosley and Mehreen Baig investigate the safety of cosmetic treatments for the body, including a procedure designed to get rid of cellulite for good, fat freezing and fat heating, and the rising use of anabolic steroids for body enhancement. The programme also examines the psychological impact of selfies on body image and puts the latest weight loss technology to the test against traditional exercise

programme.title: War on Plastic with Hugh and Anita
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Anita Rani present an update on the campaign against single use plastic, challenging a British family from Berwick to go plastic free on a budget. Hugh discovers what becomes of the packaging for pre-packed sandwiches, and Anita conducts experiments on teabags to discover the secrets of their construction. The programme also examines how the Coronavirus pandemic has led to a rise in single use plastic for safety reasons

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Documentary following people with skin conditions undergoing treatment from leading experts. The patients in the first episode are Daniel, who has had a cyst in the middle of his forehead for the past four years, Chantelle, whose acne makes her uncomfortable approaching new people, and Amy, who has a port wine stain birthmark on her face, and is an ambassador for a charity helping people to accept similar conditions

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Patients in the second episode include Reanna, who has 25 warts on her hands and the back of her knees. Ivy is self-conscious about her acne and has been hurt by negative comments in the past, and Nick has alopecia, which he conceals behind an arsenal of hats, online avatars and self-deprecating humour

programme.title: Total Wipeout: Freddie & Paddy Takeover
programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. Twenty brave men and women throw caution to the wind as they tackle a series of crazy obstacles - including the famous bouncy red balls - in a bid to make it through to the final three and the chance to win £10,000. In this edition, the contestants include a microbiologist, a PE teacher and a cheerleader

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble meets a zebra in crisis, Ben Fogle plays Sherlock Holmes to find an escaped porcupine, and Jean Johansson learns what happens when a lion comes face to face with itself

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a bungalow in Cheshire and an old chapel in Dudley ripe for development into flats. Martel Maxwell is in West Yorkshire, where the new owners of an end of terrace have grand plans for a child-friendly development

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: The latest crop of junior doctors have to put all their training and experience to the test

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams look for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly, while presenter Anita Manning investigates the history of the barber shop

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Claudia's drug project enrages Renee, and it remains to be seen how Hamid will react to Ash's announcement

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Paralympic athletes Megan Giglia, Danny Crates, Stef Reid, Dan Greaves and Jonathan Broom-Edwards compete against the general knowledge experts to win a cash prize for charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to rural Gloucestershire to help a couple of empty nesters search for a home with a £700,000 budget. While in the area, he also visits the Cotswold Gliding Club

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich create a colourful, exotic sculpture garden in a home in Llandrindod Wells for Lynn and Bill

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton make their way from Leicestershire to South Yorkshire, where the rivals try each other's tactics. Along the way, James discovers a local delicacy little known outside the region and Catherine hears the story of the most decorated soldier of the Great War - a man who never fired a gun

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Amol Rajan and Alex Jones

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Following residents of a pioneering Welsh eco-village, Brithdir Mawr, who are attempting to raise £1million to save their homes and their way of life before the lease expires. The documentary follows the community over the course of a year as they try to save their homes and preserve the eco-hamlet for posterity

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms and the memories they hold back to life, tackling a set of binoculars salvaged from a U-Boat, two boxing belts awarded to a celebrated Tongan heavyweight, a vintage mechanical money box and an old wooden high chair

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Most people who go missing are reported to police within a few hours, but for those who live on the streets it isn't always so straightforward. Aaron hasn't been seen for two months when the homeless healthcare centre that usually treats him notifies Gloucestershire Constabulary that he is a missing person. Officers scour the streets and homeless shelters but can find no trace of him. The town's rough sleepers know him well, but no one has any idea what may have happened to him. It's not long before another drug user is added to the missing priority list - James, who is of no fixed address, has discharged himself from hospital against medical advice. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Male witches arrive at Fort Salem, much to the excitement of the troops. Raelle becomes aware of disconcerting rumours about her ever-elusive girlfriend Scylla

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The base celebrates Beltane, an annual festival dedicated to love and seduction, as witches are allowed to temporarily forego military discipline and let their hair down. Supernatural drama set in an alternate America, starring Taylor Hickson and Jessica Sutton

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace recall some of the best - and the worst - reviews of the celebrities' creations. From members of the Women's Institute to previous finalists and the forthright opinions of the restaurant critics, the role of the external opinion is a crucial one. John and Gregg also revisit a classic challenge from 2011, in which the five semi-finalists were tasked with cooking the much-loved recipes from residents of a retirement village

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle joins a naughty male tiger on a training regime, Kate Humble catches up with a hand-reared cheetah and the staff search for British wildlife living freely on the estate

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts revisits a Derbyshire development project overseen by a husband and wife that he first came across in 2016. Plus, the progress of properties in Bootle and Preston

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A Welshman who relocated to Shetland is admitted to A&E with a very sore shoulder

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, Powys, as Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help the teams look for items to sell at auction

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: A heartrending death devastates some of the Towers residents. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Lifestyle presenters Piers Taylor, Tom Dyckhoff, Esme Young, Danny Clarke and Keith Brymer Jones take on the general-knowledge champions. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the south Wales countryside to help a couple with £500,000 to spend on relocating from Newport to a village community. While in the region, Nicki visits the only whisky distillery in Wales that uses the mineral-rich water of the Brecon Beacons - to potent effect

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich meet Jason and Emily, who want a bohemian fairy garden to match their 18th-century Bristol home

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton travel across Derbyshire and Leicestershire, where items of interest include 18th-century carvings and a bowl full of marbles. Along the way Catherine gets steamed up on the heritage Great Central Railway and James visits a pottery

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Jessie Wallace and Lacey Turner, better known to fans of the show as Kat and Stacey, for a Slater family reunion. They talk about their characters' most memorable moments, reveal some of their best behind-the-scenes stories and give an idea of what's to come when the soap returns next week

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back at when the celebrities have been pushed to the limits, from trying to impress some of the best chefs in the country to the most daunting challenge of all, the Chefs' Table. They also recall a couple of memorable challenges, including a final week task from 2012 in which Christine Hamilton, Dick Strawbridge and Lisa Faulkner had to prepare a fine dining lunch from a tiny kitchen on the Orient Express

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man is found murdered on his boat five miles out at sea, with no possible way a killer could have escaped. At first Humphrey turns his attention to the small group he was with the night before he died - but as the investigation deepens, the investigation takes an unexpected turn that requires the team to leave the comfort of their Caribbean island - and go searching for their suspects in London. Kris Marshall stars

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a selection of eight of the most extraordinary sequences from Planet Earth II and Blue Planet II, with a new soundtrack by Hans Zimmer, Jacob Shea and the team at Bleeding Fingers - performed by the BBC Concert Orchestra and Mercury Award-winning rapper Dave on the grand piano. The film ranges from the East Cape of South Africa to the north of Norway and from Chile to the Indian Ocean to show how animals overcome adversity to survive and thrive in some of the world's most challenging environments

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason and Adam Fleming present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Most people who go missing are reported to police within a few hours, but for those who live on the streets it isn't always so straightforward. Aaron hasn't been seen for two months when the homeless healthcare centre that usually treats him notifies Gloucestershire Constabulary that he is a missing person. Officers scour the streets and homeless shelters but can find no trace of him. The town's rough sleepers know him well, but no one has any idea what may have happened to him. It's not long before another drug user is added to the missing priority list - James, who is of no fixed address, has discharged himself from hospital against medical advice. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble meets a giraffe whose weight-loss could mean a crisis for the whole herd, while Ben Fogle finds out which eucalyptus leaves koalas like best and Jean Johansson serves up some refreshing fish lolliies for the tigers

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The makeover show returns, with Charlie Dimmock and Dave and Harry Rich working for a Bristol couple want a garden that mixes Greek plants with English ones

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don shows how to prune spring-flowering roses and makes successional sowings of vegetables to ensure continuous crops. Adam Frost gives tips helping plants cope with drought conditions and shows how to save precious water. The programme meets two sisters from Dorset who have discovered a new love of gardening, supported in their efforts by many followers on social media, and in Bognor Regis visits a gardener who has used unusual materials and bargain plants to create her own dream garden. Plus, Frances Tophill heads to Hertfordshire to meet a couple of brothers whose gardens are designed with wildlife in mind and in

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Disillusioned by life in the NHS, nurse and mum Yvonne Samson wants to move from Glasgow to Australia's Sunshine Coast, but husband David is happy to stay in Scotland

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold introduces action from the latest fixtures, including Wigan Warriors v Catalans Dragons and St Helens v Hull Kingston Rovers

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Kate Humble hosts the panel show that tests the wildlife knowledge of team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke and their guests Maya Plass and Lindsey Chapman

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Jack is found in his drunken state by Roy, who cleans him up before returning him to Ash Park. Henry and Sarah carry out the autopsy on Regina and make a startling discovery

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood's alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture continues with the letter M. Various BBC gardening presenters are mesmerised by magnolias, marvel at the monkey puzzle tree and meet a marigold enthusiast

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: A couple and their three children embark on a six-week experiment to discover how a revolution in food transformed the British way of life, guided by records of what people ate for breakfast, lunch and dinner in the decades after the Second World War. Under the guidance of journalist Giles Coren and food historian Polly Russell, they begin by sampling meals from the early 1950s, when rationing was still in effect and staples included dried eggs, national bread, dripping and liver. With a contribution by Mary Berry

programme.title: Animal Super Parents
programme.desc: Though it takes two to make a baby, it does not necessarily require two to bring it up. So what makes the parents of the animal kingdom decide to stay or go, and if they remain with what degree to involve themselves? Narrator Hugh Dennis observes creatures from a California mouse who has to kick-start her partner's paternal instincts to the Adelie penguin parents who cannot leave their eggs alone for five minutes, and from cheetah mums practising promiscuity to keep the dads on-side, to flamingo parents both producing milk for their young

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Experts James Lewis and Jonathan Pratt assess items brought to Dover Castle in Kent, while presenter Paul Martin looks at the life of James Bond creator Ian Fleming

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andi Oliver, Ivo Graham, Phill Jupitus and Kate Humble test their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week, with a winner declared each Friday

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Neil and Rhian from the Brecon Beacons and Crymych-based Mandy and Derek hope to beat the competition with their pedigree breed sheep at the Pembrokeshire County Show

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Call The Midwife co-stars Cliff Parisi and Judy Parfitt team up with regulars Catherine Southon and Phillip Serrell. Headed for an auction in Norfolk, they start their antiques hunt in Kent. Will Cliff's love for vintage toy cars secure the biggest profits or can Judy's tough bargaining skills see her triumph?

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: As autumn sets in, the farmers prepare for mating season. In Aberdeenshire, deer farmers Isla and George wrangle their herd of semi-wild deer closer to the farm to tackle the challenges of rutting season. In the Lake District, Matt and Murray invest in some new rams, while in Armadale, Joyce Campbell's 600 ewes get stuck in the mud while being herded to the hills. On the Stoer Peninsula, Helen and Graham take their small boat out during settled weather to collect their creel pots

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: The West Tower in the city centre is the latest skyscraper to open to the booming rental market. General manager Matt has over 300 apartments to rent out for owners Legal & General, and starts off with free yoga classes in the penthouse to raise the tower's profile. But as towers rise on the skyline, Salford mayor Paul Dennett needs to find a way to build more affordable housing in the city. Homeless people and low-income earners are often forced into shelters or temporary accommodation as the city's affordable housing stock falls short

programme.title: They Came from Manchester: Five Decades of Mancunian Pop
programme.desc: Compilation of BBC studio performances by some of the city's most popular bands, ranging from the 1960s to the present day. Featuring footage of archive performances by Freddie & the Dreamers, the Hollies, 10cc, Buzzcocks, the Fall, James, Joy Division, M People and Oasis

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Cricket: Today at the Test
programme.desc: Action from the third and final match in the series, which came from Emirates Old Trafford in Manchester. England had won their last five T20 series and were looking to end on a high today against a strong Pakistan side

programme.title: Best of Top Gear
programme.desc: The team looks back at highlights of the last two series of the motoring show, including Freddie Flintoff, Chris Harris and Paddy McGuinness heading to the searing heat of Ethiopia in their first cars, and Chris racing the £2.5m McLaren Speedtail against the RAF's newest fighter jet

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back on further highlights from 15 years of the show, remembering some of the most entertaining team challenges - and how different dynamics can lead to either success or chaos in the kitchen. They also revisit a 2015 semi-final task which saw Sheree Murphy, Scott Maslen, Rylan Clark-Neal, Sam Nixon, Chesney Hawkes, Mica Paris, Kimberly Wyatt and Tom Parker thrown together in a Victorian-style village, using only ingredients widely available during that era

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the 1884 murder of a policeman in Co Durham, a crime for which three men were tried but only one was hanged

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.desc: Cheetahs and endangered spiders both enter mating season. A Bactrian camel undergoes laser treatment, and a party is thrown to celebrate the ninth birthday of AJ the red panda

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and the Rich brothers create a garden in Thatcham, Berkshire, for widower Lee, who wants a space where his children can have fun and play, but also remember their mum

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Weekly magazine for the deaf and hard of hearing. With voiceover

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter checks up on her chilies and peppers in her countryside garden as well as the cucumbers in her polytunnel, and Kirsty Wilson tries out an easy-to-use propagator to make houseplants for free. Sophie McKilligan returns to her Aberdeen allotment after a break to visit family, and finds the rest of the community have kindly looked after her patch while she was away

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman follows Mwakere and Jana Mrengo from Birmingham, whose hectic lies have left them looking for a better work-life balance in New Zealand. Across one week, they see the sort of life they could lead - and afford - if they were to move there on a permanent basis

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Ferne Corrigan and Iolo Williams join team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke on the panel show that tests wildlife knowledge, with Kate Humble asking the questions

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: George and Sarah contemplate a suitable grave site for Regina, and Anna tells Olivia her plans to set up her future with Henry

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter O, continuing her alphabetical journey through the world of horticulture. Along the way, she gets inspiration and advice from some of the BBC's most popular gardening presenters

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Robshaws are transported back to the space-age 1960s, with their home having a fitted kitchen installed and a host of new tastes and flavours to be sampled. Giles Coren and Polly Russell introduce them to the culinary treats of the decade, including spaghetti bolognese and TV dinners, while Hairy Biker Dave Myers delivers their long-awaited fridge and reveals the transformative effect the appliance had on his own childhood. Giles also discovers how chicken went from an expensive treat to an everyday staple

programme.title: Animal Super Parents
programme.desc: How animals living in groups divide the labour, from elephants taking part in hostage negotiations for their babies to banded mongooses all giving birth on the same day. Also witnessed are female lions forming a sisterhood to see off male interlopers and musk oxen rallying together to defend their young from wolves. Narrated by Hugh Dennis

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antique experts James Lewis and Catherine Southon search for items to take off to auction at Dover Castle in Kent. James unearths a silver owl inkwell and Catherine discovers an alabaster bust. Presenter Paul Martin delves into the history of the English Channel

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andi Oliver, Ivo Graham, Phill Jupitus and Kate Humble test their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games this week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Neil and Rhian from the Brecon Beacons and Crymych-based Mandy and Derek hope to beat the competition with their pedigree breed sheep at the Pembrokeshire County Show

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Actresses Anna Chancellor and Holly Aird take to the antique shops of the Home Counties, ably assisted by experts Natasha Raskin Sharp and Margie Cooper. The thespian rivals come across antique lamps, glittering shoe buckles and Georgian underpants, and an eclectic collection of props ranging from a military field flask to a pigeon clock

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels by train through Austria and the Czech Republic, beginning in the northern Austrian city of Linz, where Adolf Hitler lived as a young boy. From here, Michael travels onto Prague Central Station, where he meets 87-year-old Zuzana Maresova, who, as a seven-year-old girl climbed aboard a train to travel to safety in London with hundreds of other Jewish children. Eventually crossing the Czech border into Slovakia, Michael reaches his final destination, one of Europe's youngest capital cities, Bratislava

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Lydia Quigley finds herself in court facing the newly appointed Justice Hunt, so Margaret and Nancy race to find witnesses to give evidence against their rival. Samantha Morton and Lesley Manville star

programme.title: Harlots
programme.desc: Charlotte struggles with horribly divided loyalties, while the community gathers to share its grief. As tempers flare the women rally against the injustice of the judiciary, with devastating consequences for Nancy. Samantha Morton and Lesley Manville star

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits the city of Bologna, where he calls in at the vegetable market Mercato Delle Erbe, and eats squid stuffed with potato and capers

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: The West Tower in the city centre is the latest skyscraper to open to the booming rental market. General manager Matt has over 300 apartments to rent out for owners Legal & General, and starts off with free yoga classes in the penthouse to raise the tower's profile. But as towers rise on the skyline, Salford mayor Paul Dennett needs to find a way to build more affordable housing in the city. Homeless people and low-income earners are often forced into shelters or temporary accommodation as the city's affordable housing stock falls short

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Weekly magazine for the deaf and hard of hearing. With voiceover

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges an impressionist painter, a metal sculptor and an artist who paints in a caravan to create artwork for a London couple who have just renovated their home

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Jeremy Dein QC and Sasha Wass QC re-investigate the case against farm labourer William Dumbleton, who was charged with the murder of travelling watch repairman John Edmonds in 1880. The accused man didn't have any legal representation until the commencement of his trial, which lasted just one day. Dumbleton was found guilty and sentenced to death

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble meets a zebra in crisis, Ben Fogle plays Sherlock Holmes to find an escaped porcupine, and Jean Johansson learns what happens when a lion comes face to face with itself

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich create a colourful, exotic sculpture garden in a home in Llandrindod Wells for Lynn and Bill

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: This time, Michael Portillo ventures on a rail journey through Germany, guided by Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide published in 1936. Starting in the vibrant city of Berlin, Michael visits the Museum of Modern Art in Berlin's Kreuzberg, where he sees how a leading artist of the era, George Grosz, warned of the rise of fascism in a haunting self-portrait. In the Schöneberg district of the capital, Michael researches the decadent night scene of the 1920s, and looks at how cabaret culture is being revived today. When he reaches Weimar, Michael investigates the beginnings of Bauhaus design and visits the movement's first building, before travelling onto Stuttgart, where he takes a ride in a high-performance Porsche to the manufacturer's Stuttgart headquarters

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Newlyweds the Castlings sample life in Perth for a week, but while Cheryl is keen to make a fresh start her husband Adam has begun to have doubts die to his career taking off

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Curious Creatures
programme.desc: Team captains Chris Packham and Lucy Cooke are joined by Lizzie Daly and Rachel Parris for another test of wildlife knowledge, hosted by Kate Humble

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Robshaws are transported to the 1970s and Mary Berry is on hand to help the family stock up their brand new chest freezer, as frozen and convenience food became a life saver for time-pressed working women of the decade. Giles Coren meets the two hippies whose adventures in health food boosted the popularity of houmous, while the family has a go at self-sufficiency and discovers that milking a goat is nowhere near as simple as it looks

programme.title: Back in Time for...
programme.desc: The Robshaws and their home are given a 1980s makeover, with a gadget-filled kitchen including an enormous microwave oven. Giles Coren and food historian Polly Russell use the national food survey to guide the family's diet and introduce them to the culinary treats and trends of the decade, with technology giving them the chance to make their own fizzy drinks and produce a cheese and ham toastie. They also learn about the rise of both nouvelle cuisine and the fast-food chains, and find a host of new tastes and flavours from around the globe now available at their local supermarket

programme.title: Animals Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Mongoose start wars to create a diversion for sneaky mating, while macaques kidnap babies to curry favour with their rivals. These behaviours prompt Liz Bonnin to investigate the animals who will stop at nothing to find a mate and raise a family. Liz reveals the fascinating science that lies behind some extraordinary tactics. In Tanzania, she finds out that sibling rivalry can actually benefit a whole hyena clan, and on the North American plains learns how promiscuity can be the key to having healthy prairie-dog pups. However, the presenter also discovers that such tactics are often the key to survival

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: The experts assess items brought to the Blickling estate in Norfolk, and Paul Martin learns about Lord Lothian's key role in getting America involved in the Second World War

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Andi Oliver, Ivo Graham, Phill Jupitus and Kate Humble test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: The Rev Daniel Reed from Cornwall shows his prize-winning ducks and Gabbie Franklin from Herefordshire exhibits her chickens at the Pembrokeshire County Show in Wales

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Veteran broadcasters John Sergeant and Peter White take a road trip through Surrey on a search for antiques, with expert help from James Braxton and Philip Serrell. Along the way, John takes to the river in a historic yacht and Peter - blind from birth - bats a good innings at one of the country's oldest cricket clubs

programme.title: Glow Up: Britain's Next Make-Up Star
programme.desc: The three finalists take part in their biggest professional assignment to date, delivering their own masterclass at one of the world's most famous department stores, Harrods. Taking it in turns, they must each showcase a signature look concentrating on the eyes, explaining their techniques and delivering great make-up in front of an audience of industry professionals. They next day the artists face their final creative brief, on the theme of evolution, before the judges decide which two will compete in the final face off

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: A selection of highlights from the stand-up show, featuring a different topic each week. Here, comedians Jason Byrne, Marlon Davis, Micky Flanagan, Jessica Fostekew, Guz Khan, Sean Lock and Sindhu Vee perform routines about children

programme.title: Semi-Detached
programme.desc: A journalist accompanies Kate home to write a lifestyle piece on her as the local MP. What they find is Ted and Stuart half-dressed, Madonna dressed as a tomato and everyone acting very suspiciously. Stuart tries his best to keep things together for his ex-wife, only for his own problems to arise with the return of April's former boyfriend, casting doubt over Bertha's paternity. Lee Mack stars in the real-time comedy drama, with Samantha Spiro, Patrick Baladi, Clive Russell and Christian Brassington

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's New World Order
programme.desc: The comedian tries to help make sense of bewildering times as he dissects the week's news using stand-up, review, discussion and audience interaction. Throughout the series he is joined by Sara Pascoe, Miles Jupp, Sophie Duker and a variety of other guests, beginning with Guz Khan and Sara Barron

programme.title: Comedy Shorts
programme.desc: An anxious young woman attempts to cope with the two personifications of her inner thoughts - one which encourages her to be impulsive and another that fears everything. Written by and starring Gbemisola Ikumelo

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emma Barnett

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer are joined by Feargal Sharkey for a trip to the River Lea in Hertfordshire, where Paul used to fish for perch with his friends when he was a teenager. Bob reads up on the wise words of 17th-century angler Izaak Walton and prepares a riverside picnic that brings back fond memories and leads to a reflection on the differences between teenage life in their 1970s youth and the present. Setting up home in a narrowbooat, he also prepares a dish Paul's mother used to make - corned beef and potato pie

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian presents a guide to medical dramas, looking back at shows including Emergency Ward 10, Dr Finlay's Casebook, Casualty, Angels and Doctors. Harry assesses each show for the authenticity of sets and costumes, celebrates some of the most frequently recurring tropes of the genre, and reveals what it takes to make a successful hospital drama

programme.title: Manctopia: Billion Pound Property Boom
programme.desc: Documentary about the people living and working in Manchester's remarkable housing boom. A millionaire developer plans to transform the city's red light district into a fashionable residential quarter, as well as taking charge of organising a fundraising concert for a homelessness charity. In Salford, a single mother finds herself priced out of the area she grew up in, and must find a new home for her two young children

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley and Ashley John-Baptiste discuss the personal and social benefits of spreading happiness to others, from video games in church to spending a day as a clown

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein investigate a case of starvation and neglect from 1877 that saw a husband stand trial for the murder of his wife

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: The amateur cameraman's search for red deer calves and fox cub leads to a dangerously close encounter with an animal that no one realised was living wild on Exmoor. In his home village, Bishop's Nympton, where Revel Week is in full swing, he and his friends also gather to welcome a new landlord at a local pub

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an autumnal scene, using natural shades to depict a worn-down farm building surrounded by golden grassy bushes and trees

programme.title: Ray Mears and Ewan McGregor: Extreme Jungle
programme.desc: Actor Ewan McGregor picks up a few tips from survival expert Ray Mears before heading deep into the Honduran jungle on the trail of a lost civilisation, thought to inhabit the Mosquito Coast. Guided by real-life Indiana Jones Dr Chris Begley they brave freezing conditions and biting insects, and must endure physically demanding tasks

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: The documentary charts the arrest, trial and conviction of Peter Sutcliffe, and the legacy for the relatives of his victims and the survivors of his attacks. The film explores how the trial was jeopardised when witnesses were offered money for exclusives and how the front seats in the court were reserved for VIPs. Going back to survivors of the attacks and relatives of Sutcliffe's victims, the film also explores the legacy left behind by his crimes and finds out what it has been like to live as the child of a Ripper victim

programme.title: Julius Caesar Revealed
programme.desc: Mary Beard sets out on a quest to uncover the real Julius Caesar, challenging the received popular view of the Roman politician and general. The classicist looks at how he become the autocratic ruler of Rome, the tactics of his military campaigns, his use of spin and PR on his way to the top, and the reasons behind his assassination on the Ides of March in 44 BC. The scholar also examines his surprising legacy right up to the present day, revealing how his influence is still shaping language, politics and people's everyday lives

programme.title: The Stolen Maharajah: Britain's Indian Royal
programme.desc: The story of Duleep Singh, the last Maharajah of the Punjab, who was captured by the British army as a boy in the 1840s and raised in England as an aristocrat. The young man rose through British society to become a friend of Queen Victoria - but when he realised what had been done to him, he sought to reclaim his stolen Sikh heritage and became a rebel against imperial rule in India

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Picasso is known as the godfather of modern art. Art historian David Dibosa explores six decades of BBC archives to discover how TV has influenced our understanding of him. Dibosa reveals how filmmakers have portrayed the child genius who drew like a master, and the adult who rejected his conventional talent in a quest to reflect the world in ways that shattered all the rules

programme.title: How to Get Ahead
programme.desc: Writer and broadcaster Stephen Smith explores the flamboyant court of French monarch Louis XIV at the Palace of Versailles, where he surrounded himself with aristocrats, artists, interior designers, gardeners, wig-makers, chefs and musicians. Ambitious courtiers scrambled to get closer to the king, but the bedchamber seemed to provide a more unseemly path to power

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross paints an autumnal scene, using natural shades to depict a worn-down farm building surrounded by golden grassy bushes and trees

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: The amateur cameraman's search for red deer calves and fox cub leads to a dangerously close encounter with an animal that no one realised was living wild on Exmoor. In his home village, Bishop's Nympton, where Revel Week is in full swing, he and his friends also gather to welcome a new landlord at a local pub

programme.title: The Yorkshire Ripper Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: The documentary charts the arrest, trial and conviction of Peter Sutcliffe, and the legacy for the relatives of his victims and the survivors of his attacks. The film explores how the trial was jeopardised when witnesses were offered money for exclusives and how the front seats in the court were reserved for VIPs. Going back to survivors of the attacks and relatives of Sutcliffe's victims, the film also explores the legacy left behind by his crimes and finds out what it has been like to live as the child of a Ripper victim

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: The amateur cameraman's quest to find a newborn red deer calf is interrupted by sightings of a red-legged partridge herding her brood to safety, a sparrow feeding on a privet hawk-moth and a family of stoats. Finally he visits the Exmoor Pony Centre to learn how to ride a horse

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a secret lake beneath a mountain range, surrounded by lots of trees and bushes

programme.title: Japan: Earth's Enchanted Islands
programme.desc: The documentary examining the wildlife of the east Asian country focuses on Hokkaido, Japan's northernmost island and a place totally unlike the rest of the country. Every year it swings from a bitter Siberian winter into the warmth of a Mediterranean-like summer, with the thaw revealing a landscape changed beyond all recognition. It takes hardy animals and tough people with real ingenuity to survive in this ever-changing place. Michelle Dockery narrates

programme.title: Hitler's Children
programme.desc: Documentary exploring how descendants of prominent members of the Nazi regime, including military commander Heinrich Himmler and Hitler's right-hand man Hermann Goering, have coped with the weight of their ancestors' crimes against humanity. The programme asks whether, with such tarnished family names, it has been possible to move on in life

programme.title: Lost Home Movies of Nazi Germany
programme.desc: Part one of two. Thousands of German attics have become dumping grounds for countless hours of private home movies, left gathering dust for decades and now giving an insight into the innermost recesses of life in Nazi Germany. This programme delves into these private collections, which are only now emerging, to reveal how Germans recorded the regime, featuring films from ordinary citizens as well as Nazi Party organisers, firemen, local officials and soldiers on the frontline

programme.title: Lost Home Movies of Nazi Germany
programme.desc: Part two of two. Amateur footage from the latter half of the Second World War, offering an insight into life and atrocities in Germany and the occupied territories as Hitler's dreams crumble and the moral reckoning the German people had to face. The films reveal the stories of people battling to save their families from deportation to the death camps, while others endure the horrors of ever more deadly bombing raids, all set against a backdrop of propaganda and false hope pouring forth from Nazi high command

programme.title: Art of Scandinavia
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon explores the history of the North European region through its art. He begins by examining the links between landscape, climate and national character, taking in everything from the era of the Vikings to Edvard Munch's The Scream

programme.title: People's Palaces: The Golden Age of Civic Architecture
programme.desc: Conclusion. Dr Jonathan Foyle visits some of the most impressive neo-Gothic Victorian civic buildings in the north of England. Pointed arches, spires and clustered columns are present not only on churches and cathedrals, but also town halls and libraries, and the presenter explores the influence of architect Augustus Pugin and art critic John Ruskin in transferring the style from sacred to secular constructions

programme.title: The Joy of Painting
programme.desc: Bob Ross creates a secret lake beneath a mountain range, surrounded by lots of trees and bushes

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: The amateur cameraman's quest to find a newborn red deer calf is interrupted by sightings of a red-legged partridge herding her brood to safety, a sparrow feeding on a privet hawk-moth and a family of stoats. Finally he visits the Exmoor Pony Centre to learn how to ride a horse

programme.title: Japan: Earth's Enchanted Islands
programme.desc: The documentary examining the wildlife of the east Asian country focuses on Hokkaido, Japan's northernmost island and a place totally unlike the rest of the country. Every year it swings from a bitter Siberian winter into the warmth of a Mediterranean-like summer, with the thaw revealing a landscape changed beyond all recognition. It takes hardy animals and tough people with real ingenuity to survive in this ever-changing place. Michelle Dockery narrates

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: The wildlife film-maker manages to buy the land his badger hide is on, but his excitement is overshadowed by worries about his three-legged pet deer, whose back leg is getting worse. As he tries to accept she may be nearing the end of her life, the villagers ask him to open their local flower show

programme.title: BBC Proms 2020
programme.desc: Violin superstars Nicola Benedetti and Alina Ibragimova lead a high-energy evening of baroque music by Vivaldi, Handel, Bach, and little-known English composer Charles Avison. If three double violin concertos weren't enough, there's also a double oboe concerto. With the Orchestra of the Age of Enlightenment, led by Jonathan Cohen. Presented by Danielle de Niese

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Andy Torbet braves one of the most dangerous beaches in Britain, while Nick Hewitt unearths the steel skeletons of two top-secret midget submarines at Aberlady Bay in East Lothian. Nick Crane digs deep to discover what it is like to live on one of the nation's most unusual beaches, the eerily beautiful, vast shingle spit at Dungeness in Kent

programme.title: The Damned United (2009)
programme.desc: Fact-based drama chronicling Brian Clough's ill-fated 44-day tenure as boss of Leeds United in 1974, after Don Revie's departure for the job of England's manager. Following his acrimonious split from Derby County and a brief spell at Brighton & Hove Albion, Clough arrived at Elland Road on a wave of controversy, after previously being an outspoken critic of the club, and struggled to find allies among the playing staff. With Michael Sheen, Timothy Spall, Jim Broadbent and Colm Meaney

programme.title: African Renaissance: When Art Meets Power
programme.desc: Afua Hirsch explores the cultural history of Kenya, revealing how artists, musicians and writers have responded to its colonial past and asserted a new identity through art

programme.title: Handmade in Africa
programme.desc: Following master beadmaker Jane Semanto in her Nairobi studio as she crafts a traditional Maasai wedding necklace, which is of great ceremonial significance

programme.title: Johnny Kingdom - A Year on Exmoor
programme.desc: The wildlife film-maker manages to buy the land his badger hide is on, but his excitement is overshadowed by worries about his three-legged pet deer, whose back leg is getting worse. As he tries to accept she may be nearing the end of her life, the villagers ask him to open their local flower show

programme.title: The Beauty of Maps
programme.desc: Paul Barber of the British Library discusses the golden age of Dutch cartography and examines the importance of atlases to the commercial and creative endeavours of the 17th century. As maps became a means of expressing power, wealth and control, they grew increasingly elaborate and artistic - and the documentary visits the world's largest atlas, a 24-volume example covering just the Netherlands, and the Klencke Atlas, made for Charles II's restoration in 1660

programme.title: Lost Home Movies of Nazi Germany

programme.desc: The Ninky Nonk leaves without the Tombliboos, forcing them to chase after it. Narrated by Derek Jacobi

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends enjoy a steam train adventure, and Mr Tumble zooms off into space

programme.title: Baby Jake

programme.title: Teletubbies

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The little lamb dreams of being a knight and decides to carry out a series of chivalrous deeds

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Twelve shows the other Numberblocks how to master the way of the rectangle

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Sula paint a giant mural at Amma's nursery, but Bing's rainbow colours spoil Sula's tree design and she is upset that her hard work has been ruined

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: The bunny gets stuck in Padget's shop while playing a game of hide-and-seek with Flop

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: It's winter and it's dark and too cold to play outside. The squirrels wonder what Duggee is up to - he seems to be looking through a long stick out of the window. Duggee explains that it's a telescope, and he's looking at things far away in space. The squirrels are keen to have a go too, but first Duggee tells them about the Solar System, and in order to help him they must all dress up as planets

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Harvest time arrives, and Duggee's garden is over-flowing with newly ripened crops, but there are so many it will take him forever to pick them all

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: The friends set out to taste the sweetest dandelions in the world - but they discover they are protected by the meanest rabbit in the valley

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bitz and Bob's junk stall sells out of everything, including Bob's robo-helmet! Bob is horrified

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: When a mass of poisonous sea snakes get stranded on a beach, Peso and the Octonauts must somehow get them all back into the ocean

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Q joins U to make some noise and create a vast array of new words

programme.title: Justin's House
programme.desc: Justin dresses up as Granny Justina when he and Robert forget to invite her round for a special tea party with guest Katy Ashworth

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.desc: Raa Raa hopes to show Topsy his favourite things, but they disappear - so he sets out with his friends to find the missing objects

programme.title: Timmy Time
programme.desc: The lamb cannot decide which instrument to play at band practice, so he hangs on to all of them and causes chaos at the nursery with his impromptu performances

programme.title: Dog Loves Books
programme.desc: Dog and Pug meet an elephant and a mongoose who cannot play together.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing and Flop build a sandcastle, and Pando joins them in the sandpit with his wind-up digger toy to help

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos visit an hotel and find themselves hiding behind a line of suitcases. One by one, the porter takes each suitcase away, and the Twirlywoos move down the line, hiding behind different cases. When there is only one suitcase left, the Twirlywoos have nowhere else to hide. Instead of coming out from their hiding place, they take their hiding place with them - much to the porter's confusion. Back on The Big Red Boat, a mischievous mouse entertains on the Twirlywoo Screen. Where is he hiding?

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin Fletcher spends the day at his friend's school while Mr Tumble plays 'school' with the toys

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The playful quartet cannot decide whether to have Tubby Custard or Tubby Toast for breakfast - so elect to have both

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee is painting in his favourite colouring book. The Squirrels want to have a go too - Betty wants to paint a bee that's yellow, while Tag paints a fire truck that's red, while Happy paints some water blue. However, when it's Norrie's turn she cannot find the paint colour she wants

programme.title: Andy's Dinosaur Toybox
programme.desc: Brachiosaurus throws a party to celebrate his 70th birthday.

programme.title: Let's Play

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: The feathered friend wins a trip for two to a rather exciting hotel, and finds that he settles in much quicker than Sarah

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Mo is eager to get a new picture of Tee for her wall, but Tee is more interested in pictures of bugs than posing for the camera himself. Is there any way to do both?

programme.desc: Fun and games with the 26 letters which come to life, focusing on the letter U and the words up, cup, pop, sum and mud

programme.title: Yakka Dee!

programme.title: Numberblocks

programme.title: Pablo
programme.desc: A little boy journeys from real-life into the animated art world he creates, where his friends help him handle situations that make him feel anxious

programme.title: What's the Big Idea?
programme.desc: Hugo considers all the members of his family and wonders if one day he will make his own family too

programme.title: Time for School
programme.desc: It is Remembrance Day at the school and the children make poppies to remember those who died in the wars

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Gem and her teams of Swashbucklers try to win her jewels back from pirates Cook, Line and Sinker. Game show for children

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee and the squirrels prepare to have a campfire. Duggee is getting it ready and needs the squirrels to help find lots of wood for it. He needs small sticks, medium size sticks and big sticks. The squirrels are busy collecting when Roly finds a stick with eyes that pop open and an ability to talk

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: Peter takes one of Old Brown's tail feathers, and a wild chase across the valley ensues to retrieve it and pacify the furious owl

programme.title: Andy's Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy Day travels back 70 million years to meet the miniature magyarosaurus, a relative of the giant sauropod dinosaurs, which grew no bigger than a pony. But when he arrives on their prehistoric island home he has to shrink down as well to hide from a giant flying reptile as tall as a giraffe

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie Moate uses a special thermal imaging camera to find out how the hot water system in a house works and then visits a reservoir to see where the water comes from. She also visits a factory to see how toothpaste is made

programme.title: Bitz & Bob
programme.desc: Bob is stuck in a runaway hot air balloon and needs clear instructions to land it

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world

programme.title: GiggleQuiz
programme.desc: Keith Fitt tries to juggle and quiz at the same time

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: A wrong bus later and the pair are back where they started! It's time to look at the schedule and see when their bus is due to arrive.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Umbrella is nervous about meeting up with his pen pal at the Parasol Show, so Sarah and Duck accompany him

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Mr Onion is interrupted by Little Nana and Lambkin, playing a noisy hopping game

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Small, wearing a rainbow striped scarf, catches seven coloured stones floating in space. The Iron Chicken has also found a strange metal disc something in space. Do the stones and the disc somehow fit? The answer lies in the colours of the scarf

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: Upsy Daisy, Makka Pakka, the Tombliboos and Igglepiggle go for a ride on the Pinky Ponk, but the ball bounces through the garden, making everyone slide from one end to the other

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Damian Lewis reads Peace at Last, by Jill Murphy

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Cheetahs and endangered spiders both enter mating season. A Bactrian camel undergoes laser treatment, and a party is thrown to celebrate the ninth birthday of AJ the red panda

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a property in Manchester and has some motorised fun with a toy car at a house in Liverpool. Plus, the team revisits a London project Lucy Alexander first saw three years previously. It didn't have a roof back then, but it's been worth waiting for the finish

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles starts this programme at the British Bird of Prey centre in the National Botanic Garden of Wales and asks the people of Carmarthenshire to help identify a mystery object.
