programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Ryan has a problem with three little words, and Ash shares his fears with Sully

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: A team of names from the world of food and drink takes on the general knowledge champions, as Oz Clarke, Ed Baines, Paul Rankin, Jonathan Phang and Reza Mahammad hope to win a cash prize for their chosen charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a property-searching mission in North Devon with a house buyer who has been tasked by her husband to find their perfect country home on a £600,000 budget

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Arit Anderson and the Rich brothers create a garden in Thatcham, Berkshire, for widower Lee, who wants a space where his children can have fun and play, but also remember their mum

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton embark on the second leg of their five-part expedition, heading to Worcestershire, Warwickshire and the West Midlands

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Amol Rajan and Alex Jones

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Natalie Cassidy and Shona McGarty join Stacey Dooley to talk about Sonia Fowler and Whitney Dean, reliving classic scenes and revealing juicy behind-the-scenes gossip

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Part two of two. Michael Mosley and Mehreen Baig investigate the safety of cosmetic treatments for the body, including a procedure designed to get rid of cellulite for good, fat freezing and fat heating, and the rising use of anabolic steroids for body enhancement. The programme also examines the psychological impact of selfies on body image and puts the latest weight loss technology to the test against traditional exercise

programme.title: War on Plastic with Hugh and Anita
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Anita Rani present an update on the campaign against single use plastic, challenging a British family from Berwick to go plastic free on a budget. Hugh discovers what becomes of the packaging for pre-packed sandwiches, and Anita conducts experiments on teabags to discover the secrets of their construction. The programme also examines how the Coronavirus pandemic has led to a rise in single use plastic for safety reasons

programme.title: Hell On Wheels
programme.desc: Roller derby is one of the fastest growing sports in Europe. This documentary reveals the lives and loves of Wales' first and best roller derby team Tiger Bay Brawlers, following them through the biggest moment of their sporting lives - the European Continental Championships. To the uninitiated, roller derby looks like hell on wheels - a violent spinning vortex of tattooed women on roller skates, crashing into each other at high speed. To its devotees, however, it is more than just a sport

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Documentary following people with skin conditions undergoing treatment from leading experts. The patients in the first episode are Daniel, who has had a cyst in the middle of his forehead for the past four years, Chantelle, whose acne makes her uncomfortable approaching new people, and Amy, who has a port wine stain birthmark on her face, and is an ambassador for a charity helping people to accept similar conditions

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Patients in the second episode include Reanna, who has 25 warts on her hands and the back of her knees. Ivy is self-conscious about her acne and has been hurt by negative comments in the past, and Nick has alopecia, which he conceals behind an arsenal of hats, online avatars and self-deprecating humour

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Kate Humble meets a zebra in crisis, Ben Fogle plays Sherlock Holmes to find an escaped porcupine, and Jean Johansson learns what happens when a lion comes face to face with itself

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a bungalow in Cheshire and an old chapel in Dudley ripe for development into flats. Martel Maxwell is in West Yorkshire, where the new owners of an end of terrace have grand plans for a child-friendly development

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: The latest crop of junior doctors have to put all their training and experience to the test

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Gary Pe and Nick Hall help two teams look for items in the West Sussex town of Ardingly, while presenter Anita Manning investigates the history of the barber shop

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Claudia's drug project enrages Renee, and it remains to be seen how Hamid will react to Ash's announcement

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Paralympic athletes Megan Giglia, Danny Crates, Stef Reid, Dan Greaves and Jonathan Broom-Edwards compete against the general knowledge experts to win a cash prize for charity. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin heads to rural Gloucestershire to help a couple of empty nesters search for a home with a £700,000 budget. While in the area, he also visits the Cotswold Gliding Club

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich create a colourful, exotic sculpture garden in a home in Llandrindod Wells for Lynn and Bill

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton make their way from Leicestershire to South Yorkshire, where the rivals try each other's tactics. Along the way, James discovers a local delicacy little known outside the region and Catherine hears the story of the most decorated soldier of the Great War - a man who never fired a gun

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Amol Rajan and Alex Jones

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Following residents of a pioneering Welsh eco-village, Brithdir Mawr, who are attempting to raise £1million to save their homes and their way of life before the lease expires. The documentary follows the community over the course of a year as they try to save their homes and preserve the eco-hamlet for posterity

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms and the memories they hold back to life, tackling a set of binoculars salvaged from a U-Boat, two boxing belts awarded to a celebrated Tongan heavyweight, a vintage mechanical money box and an old wooden high chair

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Most people who go missing are reported to police within a few hours, but for those who live on the streets it isn't always so straightforward. Aaron hasn't been seen for two months when the homeless healthcare centre that usually treats him notifies Gloucestershire Constabulary that he is a missing person. Officers scour the streets and homeless shelters but can find no trace of him. The town's rough sleepers know him well, but no one has any idea what may have happened to him. It's not long before another drug user is added to the missing priority list - James, who is of no fixed address, has discharged himself from hospital against medical advice. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Male witches arrive at Fort Salem, much to the excitement of the troops. Raelle becomes aware of disconcerting rumours about her ever-elusive girlfriend Scylla

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: The base celebrates Beltane, an annual festival dedicated to love and seduction, as witches are allowed to temporarily forego military discipline and let their hair down. Supernatural drama set in an alternate America, starring Taylor Hickson and Jessica Sutton

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace recall some of the best - and the worst - reviews of the celebrities' creations. From members of the Women's Institute to previous finalists and the forthright opinions of the restaurant critics, the role of the external opinion is a crucial one. John and Gregg also revisit a classic challenge from 2011, in which the five semi-finalists were tasked with cooking the much-loved recipes from residents of a retirement village

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Jon Kay and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Animal Park
programme.desc: Ben Fogle joins a naughty male tiger on a training regime, Kate Humble catches up with a hand-reared cheetah and the staff search for British wildlife living freely on the estate

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts revisits a Derbyshire development project overseen by a husband and wife that he first came across in 2016. Plus, the progress of properties in Bootle and Preston

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: A Welshman who relocated to Shetland is admitted to A&E with a very sore shoulder

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross presents from the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, Powys, as Richard Madley and Kate Bliss help the teams look for items to sell at auction

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: A heartrending death devastates some of the Towers residents. Australian drama, starring Fiona Press and Mitchell Bourke

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Lifestyle presenters Piers Taylor, Tom Dyckhoff, Esme Young, Danny Clarke and Keith Brymer Jones take on the general-knowledge champions. Hosted by Jeremy Vine

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to the south Wales countryside to help a couple with £500,000 to spend on relocating from Newport to a village community. While in the region, Nicki visits the only whisky distillery in Wales that uses the mineral-rich water of the Brecon Beacons - to potent effect

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich meet Jason and Emily, who want a bohemian fairy garden to match their 18th-century Bristol home

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton travel across Derbyshire and Leicestershire, where items of interest include 18th-century carvings and a bowl full of marbles. Along the way Catherine gets steamed up on the heritage Great Central Railway and James visits a pottery

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Amol Rajan present the magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest

programme.title: EastEnders: Secrets from the Square
programme.desc: Stacey Dooley is joined by Jessie Wallace and Lacey Turner, better known to fans of the show as Kat and Stacey, for a Slater family reunion. They talk about their characters' most memorable moments, reveal some of their best behind-the-scenes stories and give an idea of what's to come when the soap returns next week

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back at when the celebrities have been pushed to the limits, from trying to impress some of the best chefs in the country to the most daunting challenge of all, the Chefs' Table. They also recall a couple of memorable challenges, including a final week task from 2012 in which Christine Hamilton, Dick Strawbridge and Lisa Faulkner had to prepare a fine dining lunch from a tiny kitchen on the Orient Express

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man is found murdered on his boat five miles out at sea, with no possible way a killer could have escaped. At first Humphrey turns his attention to the small group he was with the night before he died - but as the investigation deepens, the investigation takes an unexpected turn that requires the team to leave the comfort of their Caribbean island - and go searching for their suspects in London. Kris Marshall stars

programme.title: Match of the Day Wales
programme.desc: Finland v Wales. Jason Mohammad presents highlights of both teams' opening fixture in Group B4 of the UEFA Nations League. With analysis from Kit Symons and Jess Fishlock

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a selection of eight of the most extraordinary sequences from Planet Earth II and Blue Planet II, with a new soundtrack by Hans Zimmer, Jacob Shea and the team at Bleeding Fingers - performed by the BBC Concert Orchestra and Mercury Award-winning rapper Dave on the grand piano. The film ranges from the East Cape of South Africa to the north of Norway and from Chile to the Indian Ocean to show how animals overcome adversity to survive and thrive in some of the world's most challenging environments

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason and Adam Fleming present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.desc: Freddie Flintoff and Paddy McGuinness provide the voiceover for classic clips from the adventure game show. In this edition, the contestants include a microbiologist, a PE teacher and a cheerleader

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Following residents of a pioneering Welsh eco-village, Brithdir Mawr, who are attempting to raise £1million to save their homes and their way of life before the lease expires. The documentary follows the community over the course of a year as they try to save their homes and preserve the eco-hamlet for posterity

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team bring four more treasured family heirlooms and the memories they hold back to life, tackling a set of binoculars salvaged from a U-Boat, two boxing belts awarded to a celebrated Tongan heavyweight, a vintage mechanical money box and an old wooden high chair

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Most people who go missing are reported to police within a few hours, but for those who live on the streets it isn't always so straightforward. Aaron hasn't been seen for two months when the homeless healthcare centre that usually treats him notifies Gloucestershire Constabulary that he is a missing person. Officers scour the streets and homeless shelters but can find no trace of him. The town's rough sleepers know him well, but no one has any idea what may have happened to him. It's not long before another drug user is added to the missing priority list - James, who is of no fixed address, has discharged himself from hospital against medical advice. Narrated by Indira Varma

programme.title: Fort Salem
programme.desc: Male witches arrive at Fort Salem, much to the excitement of the troops. Raelle becomes aware of disconcerting rumours about her ever-elusive girlfriend Scylla

programme.title: Fort Salem

programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace recall some of the best - and the worst - reviews of the celebrities' creations. programme.title: Celebrity MasterChef
programme.desc: John Torode and Gregg Wallace look back at when the celebrities have been pushed to the limits, from trying to impress some of the best chefs in the country to the most daunting challenge of all, the Chefs' Table. They also recall a couple of memorable challenges, including a final week task from 2012 in which Christine Hamilton, Dick Strawbridge and Lisa Faulkner had to prepare a fine dining lunch from a tiny kitchen on the Orient Express

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Part one of two. A man is found murdered on his boat five miles out at sea, with no possible way a killer could have escaped. At first Humphrey turns his attention to the small group he was with the night before he died - but as the investigation deepens, the investigation takes an unexpected turn that requires the team to leave the comfort of their Caribbean island - and go searching for their suspects in London. Kris Marshall stars

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough narrates a selection of eight of the most extraordinary sequences from Planet Earth II and Blue Planet II, with a new soundtrack by Hans Zimmer, Jacob Shea and the team at Bleeding Fingers - performed by the BBC Concert Orchestra and Mercury Award-winning rapper Dave on the grand piano. The film ranges from the East Cape of South Africa to the north of Norway and from Chile to the Indian Ocean to show how animals overcome adversity to survive and thrive in some of the world's most challenging environments

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg, Chris Mason and Adam Fleming present their weekly round-up from Westminster, delivering their usual mix of serious analysis and light-hearted gossip about the biggest stories in politics

programme.title: Reported Missing

Aaron hasn't been seen for two months when the homeless healthcare centre that usually treats him notifies Gloucestershire Constabulary that he is a missing person. programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: The A&E staff have to juggle space when a patient arrives with mystery abdominal pains

programme.desc: Alistair Appleton is on a property-searching mission in North Devon with a house buyer who has been tasked by her husband to find their perfect country home on a £600,000 budget

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Amol Rajan and Alex Jones

programme.title: The Truth About
programme.desc: Part two of two. Michael Mosley and Mehreen Baig investigate the safety of cosmetic treatments for the body, including a procedure designed to get rid of cellulite for good, fat freezing and fat heating, and the rising use of anabolic steroids for body enhancement. The programme also examines the psychological impact of selfies on body image and puts the latest weight loss technology to the test against traditional exercise

programme.title: War on Plastic with Hugh and Anita
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Anita Rani present an update on the campaign against single use plastic, challenging a British family from Berwick to go plastic free on a budget. Hugh discovers what becomes of the packaging for pre-packed sandwiches, and Anita conducts experiments on teabags to discover the secrets of their construction. The programme also examines how the Coronavirus pandemic has led to a rise in single use plastic for safety reasons

programme.title: Skin
programme.desc: Documentary following people with skin conditions undergoing treatment from leading experts. The patients in the first episode are Daniel, who has had a cyst in the middle of his forehead for the past four years, Chantelle, whose acne makes her uncomfortable approaching new people, and Amy, who has a port wine stain birthmark on her face, and is an ambassador for a charity helping people to accept similar conditions

programme.title: HARDtalk
programme.desc: In times of crisis, we learn about who we really are, and so it is that the global coronavirus pandemic is revealing truths about humankind and how we balance self and collective interest. Stephen Sackur speaks to writer and historian Rutger Bregman whose book Humankind: A Hopeful History is making waves around the world. Do we humans massively underestimate our capacity to change things for the better? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Wednesday 27 May.

programme.title: Macleod MP/Maclean KGB
programme.desc: Cathy MacDonald reveals the forces that shaped the lives of Soviet spy Donald Maclean and Tory cabinet minister Iain Macleod

programme.title: Trusadh (Compelling Stories)
programme.desc: In Lewis, Russian artists Tatyana, Eduard and son Sergey set about creating one of their famous moving sculptures, which they will fashion from a disused Hattersley loom

programme.title: Ceòlmhor @ Piping Live
programme.desc: Performances from the best of the 2017 festival. Fin More, Sarah Hoy and Steve Byrne combine their talents to deliver a storming session, while musical trio Alana MacInnes, Fiona Black and guitarist Shane McCartan shine with a great musical set

programme.title: Alleluia! (Spiritual Music & Verse)

programme.title: Saidheans Sporsail (Backyard Science)
programme.desc: Fun science experiments, including how to fold a container for sweets and create a dragon puppet show

programme.title: Tir is Teanga (Language and Landscape)
programme.desc: Colin MacLeod explores the language and landscape of the Cuillin, a range of rocky mountains on the Isle of Skye. Beginning his journey at Bruach na Frithe with local mountain rescuer Ewen MacKinnon, he then meets Allan Turner on a walk from Coruisk to Glen Sligachan, who explains the history of the area

programme.title: Puirt-adhair (Highland Airports)
programme.desc: Offshore helicopter pilots fly crew to oil platforms in the North Sea. There's a non-stop fuelling frenzy at Wick Airport, while the firefighters at Sumburgh Airport take part in an emergency exercise wearing their breathing apparatus

programme.title: Obair Ealain (Street Art)
programme.desc: World famous artists arrive in Scotland from all corners of the globe for one of Europe's biggest street art events - Nuart Aberdeen. Edgy, stunning, provocative, subversive; their work makes an impact. These 12 international street artists have come to paint the grey streets of Aberdeen and they have only five days to complete their work before the festival opens

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)
programme.desc: Anndra comes across a lone stranger out at sea, though her true connection to the island is unclear. Isla is keen to take things to the next level with her potential suitor

programme.title: TradFest
programme.desc: Allan MacDonald presents music from TradFest Music Festival, 2018. Among the performers are Martin Harley and Daniel Kimbro, Conor Connolly, Paudie O'Connor and Aoife O Keefe

programme.desc: Exploring the professional and personal life of the Scottish footballer

programme.desc: The true story of the infamous outlaw, questioning his reputation as the Robin Hood of the Wild West and his often overlooked history of killing unarmed victims

programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Entertainment, current affairs and fashion news, as well as showbiz stories and celebrity gossip

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: More celebrity interviews and topical debate from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revival of the supermarket-based game show in which three pairs of shoppers go wild in the aisles to win a £3,000 prize

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Zoe, Matt, Mike and Wendy pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: After reaching breaking point with being followed around by Buddy, Martin resolves to find a vet to have it put down, while Ruth finds out that Bert has been trying to make his own whisky. Clive is suspicious of Mrs Tishell and who she might be cooking meals for, Penhale makes a hash of asking Janice out, and Louisa and Martin's therapy homework results in a picnic on the beach, where they are interrupted by local vet Angela Sim - who appears to be hallucinating. Comedy drama, guest starring Caroline Quentin

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh presents the first in a series of special episodes in which he reveals the secrets behind some of his favourite garden designs, and looks back on some of the most memorable assignments featured on the show. The first episode focuses on country gardens, and revisits three past projects the team put inventive spins on - a classic country garden, a design with a cottage twist, and finally a project that set out to create an enchanted atmosphere inspired by classic children's book The Secret Garden

programme.title: Sheridan Smith: Becoming Mum
programme.desc: Documentary following the actress through the course of her pregnancy, as she reflects on her past mental health struggles and fears she may develop post-natal depression. The programme follows Sheridan and her partner as she attends her first scans and pre-natal classes, and meets a psychiatrist who specialises in mental health during and after pregnancy

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Part two of two. The documentary examines Charles's legacy for the Duchy, as senior staff prepare William for the day that he succeeds his father as the next Duke of Cornwall. The documentary also examines his well-known passion for trees and traditional rural crafts, and how these may shape the Duchy's future

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts a revival of the supermarket-based game show in which three pairs of shoppers go wild in the aisles to win a £3,000 prize

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage four of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a hilly 160.5km route from Sisteron to Orcières-Merlette. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.desc: Another helping of topical studio discussion from a female perspective, featuring celebrity interviews

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show, inviting three pairs of shoppers to 'go wild in the aisles' as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize. New quiz rounds feature alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Jamie, Steve, Abbey and Rachel pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Moira receives a call from the police informing her they have found a new witness to her hit and run, while an emotional Paul assures Vinny that nothing will force them apart again. Marlon is riled when Al stops over at the Woolpack to gloat over the Hide's takeaway service success, and Nate lashes out, smashing Jamie's car wing

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya is furious to find an alcohol delivery on the doorstep of Speed Daal and refuses point blank to let Geoff buy her out of the business, leading him to resort to sabotage. David arranges to take Shona for a walk, but it doesn't go quite to plan, while Daniel apologises to Ray for causing a scene in the bistro

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Terminal 4 turns blue, airside officer Hugh has a potential environmental mess on his hands, and an octogenarian forces herself into the country under the watchful eye of Passenger Experience Guru Demi. Narrated by Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Coronation Street Icons
programme.desc: Memorable moments and classic storylines involving the Street's eccentric cafe owner over the last 25 years, including David Neilson's thoughts on playing such a recognised character. The programme reveals David's first-ever appearance on TV in a 1976 episode of police show Z Cars and he reflects on how, at the age of 46, he was preparing to give up acting altogether when he got what he thought was going to be a small part in Coronation Street. With contributions from Julie Hesmondhalgh, Alison King, Stephanie Cole, William Roache and Sally Dynevor

programme.title: Black Work
programme.desc: Detective drama, starring Sheridan Smith as a police officer whose husband is murdered. She is expected to leave the investigation to her colleagues, but finds herself losing trust in them and goes behind their backs to track down the killer herself. However, her investigation places her and her children in jeopardy. Also starring Sharon Duce, Geraldine James and Douglas Henshall

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Quadruple Olympic gold medal-winning athlete Mo Farah opens up to the presenter about the pain he felt at being separated from his twin brother, Hassan, who stayed in Somalia when Mo emigrated to Britain as a child. He also reveals about how during the London Olympics his wife Tania planned to keep the birth of their twin daughters a secret - not just from the public, but from Mo himself. When asked about performance-enhancing drugs Mo reiterates his stance that he has always competed clean

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show, inviting three pairs of shoppers to 'go wild in the aisles' as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize. New quiz rounds feature alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage five of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a flat 183km route from Gap to Privas. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: James Martin's American Adventure
programme.desc: The chef begins the Texan leg of his journey in San Antonio, a city which was once part of Mexico. Here, James samples traditional tamales and barbecoa at the hands of the city's most famous chef, Johnny Hernandez. The chef also tries authentic street food in the form of hot, fresh chorros, and learns about the famous San Antonio Chilli Queens

programme.title: The Chase

They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale Family Tree
programme.desc: A look back at the farming family who have been right at the heart of the soap since it launched in 1972. With Annie Sugden at the helm this family have endured heartbreak and in-fighting like no other. From feuding brothers Joe and Jack to fierce sibling rivalry between Robert and Andy, it has never been easy being a Sugden

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: The Government has promised to ban the sale of all new diesel, petrol and hybrid cars in the UK by 2035. But will people - and the necessary technology - be ready for the electric revolution? Motoring journalist Ginny Buckley investigates

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: For the final leg of his journey along the Wales Coast Path, Sean Fletcher explores the sand dunes at Merthyr Mawr on horseback. He also discovers how sea cliffs breathe in and out with the pounding of waves and re-creates Marconi's first radio transmission over open water, from Lavernock Point to Flatholm island

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: This episode focuses on products that have not changed in centuries, including Heck sausages, which are produced on a family farm in Bedale, North Yorkshire. There is also a look at the Maldon salt company, who have been producing sea salt since the late 19th century, and Glenfarclas single malt whisky, which has been made by the Grant family in the Scottish Highlands since 1865

programme.title: Black Work
programme.desc: A suspect is taken into custody for Ryan's murder, but Jo is not convinced the police have the guilty party. programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Zoe, Matt, Mike and Wendy pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: After reaching breaking point with being followed around by Buddy, Martin resolves to find a vet to have it put down, while Ruth finds out that Bert has been trying to make his own whisky. Clive is suspicious of Mrs Tishell and who she might be cooking meals for, Penhale makes a hash of asking Janice out, and Louisa and Martin's therapy homework results in a picnic on the beach, where they are interrupted by local vet Angela Sim - who appears to be hallucinating. Comedy drama, guest starring Caroline Quentin

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh presents the first in a series of special episodes in which he reveals the secrets behind some of his favourite garden designs, and looks back on some of the most memorable assignments featured on the show. The first episode focuses on country gardens, and revisits three past projects the team put inventive spins on - a classic country garden, a design with a cottage twist, and finally a project that set out to create an enchanted atmosphere inspired by classic children's book The Secret Garden

programme.title: Sheridan Smith: Becoming Mum
programme.desc: Documentary following the actress through the course of her pregnancy, as she reflects on her past mental health struggles and fears she may develop post-natal depression. The programme follows Sheridan and her partner as she attends her first scans and pre-natal classes, and meets a psychiatrist who specialises in mental health during and after pregnancy

programme.title: Prince Charles: Inside the Duchy of Cornwall
programme.desc: Part two of two. The documentary examines Charles's legacy for the Duchy, as senior staff prepare William for the day that he succeeds his father as the next Duke of Cornwall. The documentary also examines his well-known passion for trees and traditional rural crafts, and how these may shape the Duchy's future

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Moira receives a call from the police informing her they have found a new witness to her hit and run, while an emotional Paul assures Vinny that nothing will force them apart again. Marlon is riled when Al stops over at the Woolpack to gloat over the Hide's takeaway service success, and Nate lashes out, smashing Jamie's car wing

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya is furious to find an alcohol delivery on the doorstep of Speed Daal and refuses point blank to let Geoff buy her out of the business, leading him to resort to sabotage. David arranges to take Shona for a walk, but it doesn't go quite to plan, while Daniel apologises to Ray for causing a scene in the bistro

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Terminal 4 turns blue, airside officer Hugh has a potential environmental mess on his hands, and an octogenarian forces herself into the country under the watchful eye of Passenger Experience Guru Demi. Narrated by Joanna Lumley

programme.title: Coronation Street Icons
programme.desc: Memorable moments and classic storylines involving the Street's eccentric cafe owner over the last 25 years, including David Neilson's thoughts on playing such a recognised character. The programme reveals David's first-ever appearance on TV in a 1976 episode of police show Z Cars and he reflects on how, at the age of 46, he was preparing to give up acting altogether when he got what he thought was going to be a small part in Coronation Street. With contributions from Julie Hesmondhalgh, Alison King, Stephanie Cole, William Roache and Sally Dynevor

programme.title: Black Work
programme.desc: Detective drama, starring Sheridan Smith as a police officer whose husband is murdered. She is expected to leave the investigation to her colleagues, but finds herself losing trust in them and goes behind their backs to track down the killer herself. However, her investigation places her and her children in jeopardy. Also starring Sharon Duce, Geraldine James and Douglas Henshall

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Quadruple Olympic gold medal-winning athlete Mo Farah opens up to the presenter about the pain he felt at being separated from his twin brother, Hassan, who stayed in Somalia when Mo emigrated to Britain as a child. He also reveals about how during the London Olympics his wife Tania planned to keep the birth of their twin daughters a secret - not just from the public, but from Mo himself. When asked about performance-enhancing drugs Mo reiterates his stance that he has always competed clean

Marlon is riled when Al stops over at the Woolpack to gloat over the Hide's takeaway service success, and Nate lashes out, smashing Jamie's car wing

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alya is furious to find an alcohol delivery on the doorstep of Speed Daal and refuses point blank to let Geoff buy her out of the business, leading him to resort to sabotage. David arranges to take Shona for a walk, but it doesn't go quite to plan, while Daniel apologises to Ray for causing a scene in the bistro

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Comedian Harry Hill narrates another selection of camcorder calamities and viewers' mobile phone footage. Featuring giddy grandmas, wedding nightmares and more below-the-belt mishaps

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: The comedian hosts a lively entertainment show featuring celebrity guests, chart-topping music and everyday people with extraordinary stories to tell

programme.title: Ellen's Game of Games
programme.desc: Volunteers from Ellen's studio audience play her outrageously fun games including Stink Tank, Oh Ship!, Blindfolded Musical Chairs and Knockin' Boots.

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: The first contestants - Llama, Miss Monster, White Tiger, Kangaroo and Turtle - perform again for guest judge Leah Remini and the rest of the panel as they are whittled down to three. Nick Cannon presents as one of the singers is unmasked and sent home.

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Three singletons go up against each other in the ultimate shopping challenge, as they try to win the affections of footballer Roger by each buying him an outfit

programme.title: Take Me Out
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness lines up another selection of potential sweethearts for his 30 single women. This time, the love lift delivers Irish dancer Mitch from Bristol, diamond consultant Adam from Essex, Salisbury-based musician Nathan, and Rick, an ice hockey player from Blackburn. Each man sets out to to impress the ladies, hoping they keep their lights on as a sign of approval, and Paddy provides an update from last week's couples on the Isle of Fernandos

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill presents another edition of his popular programme, showcasing embarrassing home-videos and malfunctioning moments, filmed and submitted by loyal viewers. Clips feature flatulent selfies, misused contraceptives and a rather distracting wedding guest

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden feels that being a billionaire clouds his interaction with the ladies, so he pretends to be an ordinary guy and immediately finds success with saleswoman Kate. Alan goes along with the masquerade, pretending to be rich, but the power goes to his head

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden, still pretending to be regular guy Sam in Kate's company, lands a job selling Christmas trees in a festive edition of the comedy. Meanwhile, Mark Zuckerberg takes an interest in Walden's business, and Berta lifts Alan's spirits

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire is reunited with a former lover when the guys travel to South Korea to retrieve the final episode of a soap he starred in while he was in the US army. Stewie is concerned that he will become a redhead

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: The Griffins struggle financially, but then Stewie suddenly becomes a star after appearing in an advertisement for peanut butter

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter and Carter go to Africa to get their money back after they are swindled in an e-mail scam, while Brian pursues Meg's friend Patty

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter forms a relationship with Evelyn, an elderly friend of his late mother - who attempts to seduce him. Meanwhile, Stewie is distraught to experience a death in the family for the first time. Guest starring Lauren Bacall

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: When players from the winning side admit their victory was a result of divine intervention, Peter and his cronies go on a worldwide quest to seek out and confront God about His messing with the outcome of the New England Patriots' football games, and thus continuing their losing streak

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan persuades his last single friend to get married so he can realise his dream of becoming a best man, but when he invites an ex-convict to the wedding, she jeopardises the event. Elsewhere, Ricky Martin causes Roger to doubt himself

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Snot is rejected after professing his love for Hayley, so Roger decides to give him a new love interest - by pretending to be Steve's female cousin. Meanwhile, Stan tries to live like a gentleman of the 1960s, complete with dark suits and dry martinis

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden feels that being a billionaire clouds his interaction with the ladies, so he pretends to be an ordinary guy and immediately finds success with saleswoman Kate. Alan goes along with the masquerade, pretending to be rich, but the power goes to his head

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden, still pretending to be regular guy Sam in Kate's company, lands a job selling Christmas trees in a festive edition of the comedy. Meanwhile, Mark Zuckerberg takes an interest in Walden's business, and Berta lifts Alan's spirits

programme.title: Iain Stirling's CelebAbility
programme.desc: Stacey Solomon leads three friends in a series of games against Katherine Ryan, Kieron Richardson, Denise Van Outen and Lauren Steadman. Hosted by Iain Stirling

programme.title: Roswell, New Mexico
programme.desc: A power cut across town leaves patients in the hospital in need of help. Liz learns more details about the night Rosa died. Sci-fi drama, starring Jeanine Mason

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: More Incredible and peculiar record-breaking attempts, featuring Australian diving pigs, the most tattooed man on the planet and dogs removing socks from people's feet. Matt Edmonds narrates

programme.title: ITV2 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mark stays with Mike and Alma for a few days. Gail gets upset with Audrey and throws her out of her house. Ida is annoyed that Sally has been promoted over her

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Derek celebrates his 60th birthday. The Duckworths urge Tricia to forget about Terry. Andy advises Liz to sell the house to Jim

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Betty is appalled by Zoe's treatment of Paddy. Vic tells Viv how hard he has been trying to find Kelly. Dee asks Rachel to be a witness at her wedding

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Vic fawns over Viv after they spend the night together. Zoe is upset at the thought of Chris becoming bankrupt. Jack shocks Sarah by selling Woodside Farm for a profit

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Merton faces a race against time to stop a saboteur when Aidensfield hosts a prestigious car hill-climb. Meanwhile, Bernie finds an old car in a cowshed and decides to renovate it, giving Vernon his chance to scoop the winnings in the event. Rural police drama, starring Duncan Bell, Peter Benson and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The Abbotts catch flu and Sid is forced to act as nursemaid. Sitcom, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: A wartime memento prompts Sid and Jean to visit Paris. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers' television breaks down, and fixing it is a question of whether the British Museum has the parts in stock. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A cleaner is found dead in her home and suspicion immediately falls upon her lodger, who is arrested and convicted of her murder. It appears to be an open-and-shut case, but Superintendent Spence begins to question the man's guilt and invites Poirot to help uncover the identity of the real killer. The Belgian sleuth teams up with crime writer Ariadne Oliver (My Family's Zoe Wanamaker) as he tries to crack the case. Whodunit, starring David Suchet, with Richard Hope, Joe Absolom, Amanda Root, Sian Phillips and Raquel Cassidy

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A seemingly straightforward burglary investigation takes an unexpected turn when the victim is found to be leading a double life and suspicions are raised that the break-in could have been part of an insurance fraud. Blaketon falls for the charms of a solicitor and teams up with Ventress to help her track down the beneficiaries of an unusual bequest. Rural police drama, starring Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The Sugdens look forward to moving into their new farm. Eric asks Zak to be his best man. Dee's hen night ends in tears after the other ladies share secrets about Pollard

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The stag night prank played on Eric makes him late for his wedding, and then his honeymoon has an unexpected hitch. The Sugdens have a meeting with Andy's teacher

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Mavis tries to make amends with Derek. The Platts discover Nick is in Leeds. Jack gives Fred free drinks in the hope of being made a Square Dealer

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Kevin finds his new job boring while Sally enjoys running the factory. Des and Claire start their boating holiday and Becky changes her mind about not coming with them

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A seemingly straightforward burglary investigation takes an unexpected turn when the victim is found to be leading a double life and suspicions are raised that the break-in could have been part of an insurance fraud. Blaketon falls for the charms of a solicitor and teams up with Ventress to help her track down the beneficiaries of an unusual bequest. Rural police drama, starring Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Musicians compete for a prestigious award at the annual Thassingham Classical Music Festival - and Barnaby and Winter are called in when the winner is brutally murdered and his priceless violin is stolen. Suspicion rapidly falls on the maestro organising the festival (James Fleet), who had planned to debut his new composition at the event and has stirred up resentment by turning a community festival into a corporate one - while many suspect that the murder is the result of a curse. However, a drunken viola player (Simon Callow) turns out to have some dark secrets that may implicate him in the crime

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A murder inquiry is launched when the police are alerted to shocking internet footage showing a teenage girl being attacked and shot. The investigation is hindered by the lack of a body, but the video provides enough clues to eventually put a name to the victim and track down her killers. However, a vow of silence between the defendants means Jake and Alesha face one of their toughest court battles yet

programme.title: Cleaning Up
programme.desc: Sam's money-making plan starts to bear fruit, but it comes at a great cost. Frustrated by her mum's constant absence and the new lodger living in her bedroom, Alice seeks refuge at her dad's house. Later, Sam's listening device is found in the office, and an investigation is launched which cuts off their source of information. Desperate to make amends with Jess and bring in some money, Sam follows an inside lead and enlists the help of a fellow cleaner, Mina, to execute a risky scam in another office

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A homicidal rapist escapes from prison and embarks on a deadly game of cat and mouse with Morse, relying on his mastery of disguise and bizarre occult connections to stay one step ahead of the law. Only the madman's psychiatrist seems able to provide a lead as to his whereabouts - and her life is in mortal danger as a result. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Keith Allen, Richard Griffiths and Harriet Walter

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean encounters an old flame, prompting a jealous Sid to remember why he married her in the first place. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Poor George feels outnumbered when he learns Mildred's mother could be moving in. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A girl disappears, prompting the police to begin a desperate search, convinced her errant father has snatched her. Blaketon decides to lend his expertise to the case - only to end up fighting for his life. Meanwhile, Crane and Bellamy's dedication to duty sees them placing their lives on the line to rescue the youngster - but tragedy strikes. James Carlton, Mark Jordon and Derek Fowlds star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emma goes back to school for the first time since Geri's birth. Tony is in a rush to complete the purchase of Woodside Farm. Steve advises Frank to sell some of his assets

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve apologises to Zoe. Biff prepares to see his dad for the first time. Sophie and Zoe's relationship grows stronger, but there is an unexpected shock in store for them

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Roy urges Gail to think positively about finding Nick. Josie is appalled when Don tells her they might lose all their money over the garage. Liz finds a new flat to rent

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ida tries to cause trouble for Sally. Becky starts to enjoy being with Des and Claire. Jim is upset to discover Andy is planning to move out and live with Anne

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A girl disappears, prompting the police to begin a desperate search, convinced her errant father has snatched her. Blaketon decides to lend his expertise to the case - only to end up fighting for his life. Meanwhile, Crane and Bellamy's dedication to duty sees them placing their lives on the line to rescue the youngster - but tragedy strikes. James Carlton, Mark Jordon and Derek Fowlds star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oxford's last-surviving all-female college is holding a reunion to bid farewell to one of its most prominent professors. However, the evening leads to tragedy when a guest is found murdered, and Lewis is convinced the killing is connected to an attack that occurred at the same institution 10 years previously. The detective calls in his former DS, Alison McLennan, to help with the investigation, but the case takes a twist when two more bodies turn up. Crime drama, guest starring Juliet Stevenson and Saskia Reeves, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The team tries to juggle three cases over the course of one day, beginning at 6am when Brooks and Casey are called to a crime scene where a barman has been shot dead in his car. Then, a family dispute leads to murder, and the frenetic morning takes an unexpected turn when an allegation of police intimidation is made against one of the officers. Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls star

programme.title: Cleaning Up
programme.desc: While the investigation continues at the office, Sam and Jess are interrogated by the firm's compliance team. Jess admits to Sam that she has made a shocking discovery about Blake. He left a phone in his office containing details of his illegal trades and linking the women to his insider network. Meanwhile, Alice starts to learn more about her parents' separation and her mother's addiction, and Dave drops a bombshell which further threatens Sam's relationship with the girls. Starring Sheridan Smith and Jade Anouka

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An opera singer is shot by a sniper at an Oxford University ceremony just as she is about to receive an honorary degree. Morse is a big fan of her work and thinks she could not possibly be involved in anything untoward, but his investigations turn up more than a few sordid secrets. Detective drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Robert Hardy, Sheila Gish, John Gielgud and Rachel Weisz

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sally considers a job which will take her away from the family home. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid believes he is in for a fortune when a friend mentions him in his will. Comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George seeks solace when his budgie dies, but Mildred is too preoccupied with finding something good enough to donate to the jumble sale. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A new recruit reports for his first day of work at the police station, but given his family's history in Aidensfield, not all of the villagers are pleased to see him. Meanwhile, Ben and Liz's fund-raising efforts appear to have rubbed off on Vernon, who begins to show his charitable side

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank explains to Chris and Zoe why he has been released from prison. Viv is fed up of looking after the business. Zoe arranges a date with Sophie

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Linda does not realise why Biff is upset. The Dingles are fed up of Albert's cooking. Chris witnesses Zoe say a fond farewell to Sophie when she leaves for Zurich

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Sean warns Des that Claire probably just wants a father for Becky. Kevin informs Tony he wants to buy the garage. Jim tells Steve he is going to set up a business for them

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tony and Kevin celebrate when the bank accepts their offer on the garage. Gail receives an alarming call from the police. Emily is nearly knocked over by Samantha on her motorbike

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A new recruit reports for his first day of work at the police station, but given his family's history in Aidensfield, not all of the villagers are pleased to see him. Meanwhile, Ben and Liz's fund-raising efforts appear to have rubbed off on Vernon, who begins to show his charitable side

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the murder of a businesswoman connected with an organisation that supports gifted children, who had apparently been blackmailing the father of one particularly promising youngster. Further deaths follow, and as the body count rises, they realise there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case. Matters are complicated still further by the presence in Oxford of former MI5 spymaster Grace Orde, who is visiting to sign copies of her tell-all memoir - which provides a vital clue to the killer's identity. Detective drama, guest starring Anna Chancellor and Cherie Lunghi, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A police lab technician is stabbed to death in her home and while Brooks and Casey pursue their lead suspect, another woman is attacked in an identical fashion. The second victim survives and is able to identify her assailant, but she and Casey fall for each other - a relationship that threatens to damage both the CPS's case and the detective's career. Guest starring Lydia Leonard, with Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Cleaning Up
programme.desc: Terrified that her identity will be revealed, Sam navigates her way through the Network meeting. Promising Swanny that she can fill Blake's shoes, she is left with the impossible task of acquiring secret information about an upcoming property deal. Sam ropes in Jess and Mina to help with her biggest scam yet, but things do not go according to plan. Elsewhere, Sam deals with pressures at home, as she tries to prove to her daughters how much she truly cares for them. Starring Sheridan Smith and Jade Anouka

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A man accused of murdering five people is killed in prison shortly before going to trial, wrapping up a lot of loose ends and saving the state the expense of keeping him behind bars indefinitely. Alas, Morse cannot help wondering why the body of one of his victims was never found - and the chance discovery of a corpse gives him the perfect opportunity to investigate further. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman

Blaketon falls for the charms of a solicitor and teams up with Ventress to help her track down the beneficiaries of an unusual bequest. Rural police drama, starring Derek Fowlds

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: English country squire Marcus Waverly receives a series of anonymous letters threatening to kidnap his son Johnnie, but the case is dismissed by Scotland Yard - prompting Poirot to open his own investigation. Drama, starring David Suchet and Geoffrey Bateman

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Musicians compete for a prestigious award at the annual Thassingham Classical Music Festival - and Barnaby and Winter are called in when the winner is brutally murdered and his priceless violin is stolen. Suspicion rapidly falls on the maestro organising the festival (James Fleet), who had planned to debut his new composition at the event and has stirred up resentment by turning a community festival into a corporate one - while many suspect that the murder is the result of a curse. However, a drunken viola player (Simon Callow) turns out to have some dark secrets that may implicate him in the crime

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A murder inquiry is launched when the police are alerted to shocking internet footage showing a teenage girl being attacked and shot. The investigation is hindered by the lack of a body, but the video provides enough clues to eventually put a name to the victim and track down her killers. However, a vow of silence between the defendants means Jake and Alesha face one of their toughest court battles yet

programme.title: Cleaning Up
programme.desc: Sam's money-making plan starts to bear fruit, but it comes at a great cost. Frustrated by her mum's constant absence and the new lodger living in her bedroom, Alice seeks refuge at her dad's house. Later, Sam's listening device is found in the office, and an investigation is launched which cuts off their source of information. Desperate to make amends with Jess and bring in some money, Sam follows an inside lead and enlists the help of a fellow cleaner, Mina, to execute a risky scam in another office

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A homicidal rapist escapes from prison and embarks on a deadly game of cat and mouse with Morse, relying on his mastery of disguise and bizarre occult connections to stay one step ahead of the law. Only the madman's psychiatrist seems able to provide a lead as to his whereabouts - and her life is in mortal danger as a result. John Thaw stars, with Kevin Whately, Keith Allen, Richard Griffiths and Harriet Walter

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective begins to suspect something is amiss when he visits one of his favoured haunts in London for dinner and discovers a fellow regular has dramatically changed his diet. Down in Brighton, elderly Anthony Gascoigne lies dying while his estranged twin brother Henry falls to his death - but nobody is sure whether the man was deliberately pushed. Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean encounters an old flame, prompting a jealous Sid to remember why he married her in the first place. Seventies comedy, starring Sid James and Diana Coupland

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Poor George feels outnumbered when he learns Mildred's mother could be moving in. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: Schoolgirl Jennifer Sutcliffe becomes embroiled in a mystery when priceless jewels are hidden among her belongings. The precious stones once belonged to a prince who fell foul of a revolution, and now his cousin is a pupil at the same school as Jennifer, where Poirot finds himself drawn into the unlikely situation and realises nothing is quite what it seems. David Suchet stars as the Belgian sleuth, with Harriet Walter and Claire Skinner

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A girl disappears, prompting the police to begin a desperate search, convinced her errant father has snatched her. Blaketon decides to lend his expertise to the case - only to end up fighting for his life. Meanwhile, Crane and Bellamy's dedication to duty sees them placing their lives on the line to rescue the youngster - but tragedy strikes. James Carlton, Mark Jordon and Derek Fowlds star

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emma goes back to school for the first time since Geri's birth. Tony is in a rush to complete the purchase of Woodside Farm. Steve advises Frank to sell some of his assets

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Steve apologises to Zoe. Biff prepares to see his dad for the first time. Sophie and Zoe's relationship grows stronger, but there is an unexpected shock in store for them

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Roy urges Gail to think positively about finding Nick. Josie is appalled when Don tells her they might lose all their money over the garage. Liz finds a new flat to rent

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ida tries to cause trouble for Sally. Becky starts to enjoy being with Des and Claire. Jim is upset to discover Andy is planning to move out and live with Anne

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: In Dover, Lieutenant Colin Race is investigating the deaths of two Navy personnel when a distraught Sheila Webb runs out of 19 Wilbraham Crescent and into his arms. Inside the house, Colin finds the body of a murder victim surrounded by stopped clocks. Mystery, starring David Suchet, Anna Massey, Jaime Winstone, Lesley Sharp, Phil Daniels, Geoffrey Palmer and Tessa Peake-Jones

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

Bizarrely, the key to the riddle seems to lie in a blackberry pie. David Suchet stars in the title role, with Philip Jackson, Hugh Fraser and Pauline Moran

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oxford's last-surviving all-female college is holding a reunion to bid farewell to one of its most prominent professors. However, the evening leads to tragedy when a guest is found murdered, and Lewis is convinced the killing is connected to an attack that occurred at the same institution 10 years previously. The detective calls in his former DS, Alison McLennan, to help with the investigation, but the case takes a twist when two more bodies turn up. Crime drama, guest starring Juliet Stevenson and Saskia Reeves, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The team tries to juggle three cases over the course of one day, beginning at 6am when Brooks and Casey are called to a crime scene where a barman has been shot dead in his car. Then, a family dispute leads to murder, and the frenetic morning takes an unexpected turn when an allegation of police intimidation is made against one of the officers. Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls star

programme.title: Cleaning Up
programme.desc: While the investigation continues at the office, Sam and Jess are interrogated by the firm's compliance team. Jess admits to Sam that she has made a shocking discovery about Blake. He left a phone in his office containing details of his illegal trades and linking the women to his insider network. Meanwhile, Alice starts to learn more about her parents' separation and her mother's addiction, and Dave drops a bombshell which further threatens Sam's relationship with the girls. Starring Sheridan Smith and Jade Anouka

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: An opera singer is shot by a sniper at an Oxford University ceremony just as she is about to receive an honorary degree. Morse is a big fan of her work and thinks she could not possibly be involved in anything untoward, but his investigations turn up more than a few sordid secrets. Detective drama, starring John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Robert Hardy, Sheila Gish, John Gielgud and Rachel Weisz

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.desc: Sean warns Des that Claire probably just wants a father for Becky. Kevin informs Tony he wants to buy the garage. Jim tells Steve he is going to set up a business for them

programme.desc: Tony and Kevin celebrate when the bank accepts their offer on the garage. Gail receives an alarming call from the police. Emily is nearly knocked over by Samantha on her motorbike

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The detective's friend Sir Charles Cartwright (guest star Martin Shaw) hosts a cocktail party at his home in Cornwall, where an amiable local priest chokes to death on his drink. There seems to be no motive for murder, and no trace of poison found in the victim's glass, but weeks later, when Sir Charles's doctor friend Sir Bartholomew Strange dies in similar circumstances, he and Poirot team up to investigate. Drama, with Art Malik, Jane Asher and Anna Carteret, and David Suchet in the title role

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A new recruit reports for his first day of work at the police station, but given his family's history in Aidensfield, not all of the villagers are pleased to see him. programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: After enjoying a trip to the theatre with Captain Hastings, the detective is disturbed by the sound of gunshots in an apartment at Whitehaven Mansions, and soon finds himself investigating a murder. Drama, starring David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway and Lewis investigate the murder of a businesswoman connected with an organisation that supports gifted children, who had apparently been blackmailing the father of one particularly promising youngster. Further deaths follow, and as the body count rises, they realise there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case. Matters are complicated still further by the presence in Oxford of former MI5 spymaster Grace Orde, who is visiting to sign copies of her tell-all memoir - which provides a vital clue to the killer's identity. Detective drama, guest starring Anna Chancellor and Cherie Lunghi, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: A police lab technician is stabbed to death in her home and while Brooks and Casey pursue their lead suspect, another woman is attacked in an identical fashion. The second victim survives and is able to identify her assailant, but she and Casey fall for each other - a relationship that threatens to damage both the CPS's case and the detective's career. Guest starring Lydia Leonard, with Bradley Walsh and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Cleaning Up
programme.desc: Terrified that her identity will be revealed, Sam navigates her way through the Network meeting. Promising Swanny that she can fill Blake's shoes, she is left with the impossible task of acquiring secret information about an upcoming property deal. Sam ropes in Jess and Mina to help with her biggest scam yet, but things do not go according to plan. Elsewhere, Sam deals with pressures at home, as she tries to prove to her daughters how much she truly cares for them. Starring Sheridan Smith and Jade Anouka

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A man accused of murdering five people is killed in prison shortly before going to trial, wrapping up a lot of loose ends and saving the state the expense of keeping him behind bars indefinitely. Alas, Morse cannot help wondering why the body of one of his victims was never found - and the chance discovery of a corpse gives him the perfect opportunity to investigate further. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star
