programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: In an earlier investigation, Jeremy Wade was propelled into an Indian river by a goonch catfish as big as himself, yet managed to land the rare beast after an epic battle. Ten years on, Jeremy hears a report from neighbouring Nepal of a mysterious attack that sounds strangely familiar - a lone fisherman on a mountain river dragged off his feet and into the water by an unseen creature. The extreme angler contemplates whether the original river monster could be back, and sets off to find out

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: In an earlier investigation, Jeremy Wade was propelled into an Indian river by a goonch catfish as big as himself, yet managed to land the rare beast after an epic battle. Ten years on, Jeremy hears a report from neighbouring Nepal of a mysterious attack that sounds strangely familiar - a lone fisherman on a mountain river dragged off his feet and into the water by an unseen creature. The extreme angler contemplates whether the original river monster could be back, and sets off to find out

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits some of the restaurants that he set out to save, including The Mixing Bowl, The Olde Stone Mill, Peter's and Campania. Have their owners pulled themselves out of their nightmares?

programme.title: River Cottage
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall sets out to prove that breakfast is the most important meal of the day. He visits a school to demonstrate how to make pancakes and scones, and heads to Bristol's main train station to show commuters how to start the day in style. A canteen event also showcases a reinvention of the full English breakfast, featuring mushroom soup with brown sauce and bacon rind, and builder's tea granita

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley host the words-and-numbers game in which contestants pit their wits against the clock. Dr Linda Papadopoulos is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Ben Hillman helps a Salisbury couple who visit Malta three times a year and hope to move there permanently to find a holiday home on the island for a budget of £160,000

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Belfast, three couples compete to host the best dinner party. Sarah and Paddy kick things off with a fine-dining feast for guests Louise and Danny and Emma and Samson. The hosts mix Eastern and traditional fare. Then, on night two Emma and Samson treat their guests to some Mozambican delights. After a first course that whistles and wows, palates need to be cleansed for the main course, but a chilly chat about ice cream leaves Louise red in the face. On the final night, Louise and Danny confound everyone's expectations with a sailing-based starter. A spot of model behaviour excites the guests, and a mountain of meringue impresses, but is it enough to win the £1,000 prize?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart has the bright idea of taking out a credit card in Santa's Little Helper's name and orders a fully trained pedigree collie. Unfortunately, he has trouble keeping up the payments and soon receives a visit from bailiffs wanting to repossess all his purchases

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: As Principal Skinner prepares to enjoy a ceremony celebrating his 20th year as head of Springfield Elementary, a stranger exposes him as a former street punk who stole his identity. Featuring the guest voice of Martin Sheen

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: The team looks back at snack-based stories from past episodes. Jimmy Doherty investigates unusual shaped crisps, Matt Tebbutt discovers the dangers of double-dipping tortilla chips, Helen Lawal reports on whether rice cakes are as good a diet snack as commonly believed, and Kate Quilton delves into the world of macadamia nuts in Hawaii, and uncovers the reasons for rising numbers of children with peanut allergies

programme.title: Call the Cops
programme.desc: A look at the challenges the Devon and Cornwall police force face patrolling the 730 miles of coastline in their jurisdiction. Cornwall is famous for a long history of smuggling, but this criminal activity is not a thing of the past. In this episode, the police control room receives a call from a member of the public on Friday morning, claiming unusual cargo is being unloaded from a tiny boat into a waiting van. Soon 30 police units from across both counties have been deployed, but a shocking discovery is made when the vehicle is tracked down

programme.title: 999: What's Your Emergency?
programme.desc: This edition focuses on the policing of roads in Northamptonshire, which has borne more than its fair share of funding cuts. This has resulted in fewer police officers available to attend a huge range of traffic issues, from the routine to the surreal, on the county's 3,000-mile road network, as well as a spike in crime

programme.title: Shameless
programme.desc: When a child goes missing from a party, Frank's desire to help turns even the ice-cream man into a suspect. To make matters worse, the youngster is found with nine-year-old tearaway Debbie - leaving the Gallaghers with the challenge to get the kid back. Drama series, starring David Threlfall, Anne-Marie Duff and Rebecca Ryan

programme.title: Crime and Punishment
programme.desc: This episode of the documentary follows Hampshire Police's Chief Constable Olivia Pinkney and her officers, as they tackle offending in two areas of serious crime - domestic violence and historic sex offences. In Southampton, Detective Sergeant Will Whale, who's been with the police for more than 30 years, is leading the team navigating the domestic abuse cases. Meanwhile, Detective Constable Paul Longhurst is working toward the trial of a PE teacher accused of sexually abusing a number of his pupils

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Baltimore, Maryland, where Denise Whiting has alienated customers by trademarking the word 'hon', a term of endearment used by the local community. When she opened Cafe Hon in 1992 it was a roaring success, but as a result of Denise's unwavering business ambition, people started to boycott the restaurant, leaving her frustrated by falling sales

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA

programme.title: This Is Us

Meanwhile, Randall struggles under the pressure of both his work and his ailing father, and Rebecca and Jack's Valentine's Day doesn't go as planned. Starring Mandy Moore and Milo Ventimiglia

programme.title: Dirty Secrets of Britain's Takeaways
programme.desc: Each year, five million people in Britain get serious food poisoning after eating takeaways, but it seems most of are too busy to check the hygiene ratings before ordering. Chef April Jackson and food hygiene specialist Gareth Jones set out on a mission to clean up the nation's fast-food outlets, one restaurant at a time. Gareth joins council food inspectors behind the kitchen doors and discovers filth, dangerous levels of bacteria and evidence of rodent infestations, while he and April choose one dirty establishment to transform into a pristine 5-star hygiene rating restaurant.

programme.title: Car S.O.S.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: A trio of couples try to outdo each other in Aberdeen, coming up with an Ibizan fiesta, a vegetarian-influenced menu, and an evening of posh nosh and dinner party classics

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa takes pity on a bankrupt Mr Burns and encourages him to build a recycling centre, hoping it will get him back on his feet - but makes the mistake of underestimating his ruthlessness. Featuring the guest voice of wrestler Bret Hart

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart angrily breaks Lisa's treasured saxophone during a petty squabble, prompting Homer and Marge to recall how she acquired it in the first place. Guest starring the voice of Fyvush Finkel

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: More of the most memorable moments from the past five years, including one of the zoo's most enduring love stories at Penguin Island. At 22, Rudd is the oldest penguin on the island and has been with his partner Spike for more than 15 years. Meanwhile, Silvery gibbon Alvin is keen to get close to his partner Tilu again, but ever since they had a baby together, young Eko has proven a bit of a passion killer

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Return of the documentary following life inside an A&E department. The series begins with the story of 22-year-old police officer Hazel, who is rushed to St George's after crashing her motorbike while out on a ride with her father. Meanwhile, 86-year-old John is admitted to A&E with a suspected 'triple A' - a ruptured aortic aneurysm in his abdomen, a condition that can be fatal. The doctors must work quickly to diagnose the cause of the internal bleeding, but their investigations reveal further complications. John's children share the story of their father's life, and his surprising secret that sent shockwaves through the family

programme.title: Inside Missguided: Made in Manchester
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes at one of Britain's biggest online fashion retailer's offices to explore what life is like for their young, female-dominated workforce at their offices in the heart of Manchester. In the final episode, cameras follow the Missguided team as they prepare for one of the most important days of the year, Black Friday. To hit their targets, the brand are launching their first ever ski wear range, and lead creative Treasure is sent to Austria for a photoshoot in sub-zero temperatures

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Two daters returning to the restaurant include recruitment consultant Jack, 27, who is unwilling to change for anyone. Will he measure up for 21-year-old hairdresser Shauna? Meanwhile, 86-year-old Douglas and 76-year-old Joan bond over a shared love of musicals, and social worker Becky 36, meets blockbuster movie safety officer Dave during her second visit

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay's next stop is Philadelphia, where family run restaurant Chiarella's is struggling to survive in an area full of thriving Italian eateries. Tommy and Dina Defino's marriage is on the verge of collapse and his sense of personal failure only adds to the troubles of their crisis-ridden business

programme.title: Lodgers for Codgers
programme.desc: The chances of a 25 to 35-year-old person owning a home have halved in the last 20 years, while millions of over-sixties are sitting on empty rooms, so here young and old tackle Britain's housing crisis by trialling a unique solution - moving in together. In this first episode, 19-year-old manager Liam moves in with 83-year-old straight-talker Flo in Brighton, while Londoner Nicole heads to Hastings to live with Claudine and Ted. How will Liam cope with his first taste of independence, what will Flo make of a night out on the town, and can Claudine and Ted succeed in showing Nicole a more affordable way to live?

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Notting Hill, West London, Queen and Shawn kick off the culinary competition with a menu inspired by their Thai and West Indian heritage, but well-travelled foodies Suzy and Paul are unimpressed. When one of the hosts reveals a dislike of West Londoners, the guests are left stunned, while day two sees Suzy and Paul prepare a French feast, but Shawn sees the evening as pretentious. Finally, fun-loving couple Charlotte and Kevin put together a trendy Japanese menu, before the winners of the £1,000 prize money are revealed

programme.title: Fun Size (2012)
programme.desc: A teenage girl looking forward to a Halloween party has her hopes dashed when her mother insists she take her little brother trick-or-treating instead. programme.desc: Amy is caught in a battle of wills between Sheldon and Penny, while Leonard tries to reignite his long-distance relationship with Priya, who has moved back to India. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki, Kaley Cuoco and Jim Parsons

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard inadvertently gives the impression he is attracted to Amy when he accompanies her to a colleague's wedding. Meanwhile, Howard tries to persuade Bernadette they should live with his mother after getting married and suggests a trial run of the arrangement over the weekend

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Andy Wiederhorn, CEO of Los Angeles-based fast food restaurant Fatburger, goes incognito among his employees in order to learn more about his business that has evolved from a late-night hangout for musicians in the 1950s to an international success story

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Includes headlines and weather

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for West Babylon, New York, to help the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts. The chef struggles to cope with disorganised staff and an unappealing menu, but quickly realises the real problem lies in the kitchen, where a lack of leadership and communication is causing serious trouble

programme.title: River Cottage
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall champions the baking of bread, with recipes ranging from simple white loaves to tangy sourdoughs. He also shows how to turn leftovers into a three-course breadcrumb dinner and prepares his favourite summer sandwich. Pam Corbin reveals the secrets of making jam, and Steve Lamb demonstrates how to build a pizza oven for a garden

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Nick Hewer and Rachel Riley present the words-and-numbers game, with guest Paul Zenon alongside Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman helps Steve and Jane from Eastbourne buy their first property together on the island of Tenerife, locating five options within their budget of £200,000

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples in north London compete to host the best dinner party. First to host are husband-and-wife team Elorm and Mimi, who are confident their Afro-fusion menu and some Afro-beats will see them triumph. Night two sees Kiri and Dale host a soiree that includes an introduction to the host's love of taxidermy. On the final night, hosts Serge and Eleni treat their guests to a menu originating from their Greek-Cypriot heritage. Their twist is a somewhat unique 'Kebab Olympics' in the back garden

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge begins work as a church volunteer and restores Reverend Lovejoy's faith in his vocation, while Homer is baffled to discover his image printed on a box of Japanese washing powder

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Trio of scary short stories. Homer is left battling against killer mutants in the ruins of a devastated Springfield. An accident with a matter-transportation device causes Bart to switch bodies with a housefly, and in colonial times Marge is accused of witchcraft

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Including sport and weather

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer look back at homes by the sea as they continue to celebrate two decades of the property search programme. Inyang and Gregor have sold up in Glasgow and want to move somewhere they have never even visited - Cornwall. London fashion designer Shane has his heart set on the Kent coast, young-at-heart pensioners Peggy and Jon are swapping Derby for the Norfolk coast and Jemma and Graham, who moved back from LA, are struggling to find their dream seaside home in Essex

programme.title: Council House Britain
programme.desc: Documentary following frontline council workers and residents in Southwark, south London, one of the biggest housing associations in the country. Housing officer Charmain helps a man who has lost his key, deals with a window cleaner performing a high-wire act and worries about a vulnerable resident who is not answering his door. Andy from pest control tackles an empty flat with a fridge full of cockroaches

programme.title: Crime and Punishment
programme.desc: The documentary follows the Crown Prosecution Service and detectives from Hampshire's Major Crime team as they struggle to prosecute a man accused of killing a 13-year-old schoolgirl - a case made more difficult by budget cuts. The film also follows a Southampton detective and CPS lawyer as they team up to charge a man accused of exposing himself to a six-year-old boy

programme.title: Ramsay's 24 Hours to Hell and Back
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay is in Wichita, Kansas, where he tries to revitalise Fetch Bistro, which offers diners a place to eat alongside their canine companions. The chef and his team have just 24 hours to update the interior and pare down the menu and teach the staff the new dishes. They plan a pared-down menu featuring new dishes and a more consistent approach, with their customers asked to come back the next day to assess the changes. What will they think of the improvements to the restaurant?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three previous restaurants he tried to save, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he heads for West Babylon, New York, where he helped the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts, before returning to Davide in Boston, an Italian restaurant run by two warring brothers. Finally, he visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island, run by hot-headed owner Abby

programme.title: The Yorkshire Jobcentre
programme.desc: Lisa, 38, has just been released from prison and is going for an interview as a work coach at the Jobcentre, and hopes this will give her the second chance she desperately needs. Ex-heroin-user Wayne is desperate to turn his life around and has been referred to work coach Pip on the social justice team, while 48-year-old Justin has worked for many years in marketing and media, but has turned to the public employment service for advice about becoming a self-employed taxi driver

programme.title: Inside Missguided: Made in Manchester
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes at one of Britain's biggest online fashion retailer's offices to explore what life is like for their young, female-dominated workforce at their offices in the heart of Manchester. In the final episode, cameras follow the Missguided team as they prepare for one of the most important days of the year, Black Friday. To hit their targets, the brand are launching their first ever ski wear range, and lead creative Treasure is sent to Austria for a photoshoot in sub-zero temperatures

Realising that this bolshie bulldog's behaviour is all for attention, Graeme sets about turning it around and to restore harmony to the home. Later, in Northampton, Graeme encounters Pomeranians Bella and Lola, who are attacking anything coming through the door

programme.title: The Yorkshire Vet
programme.desc: A one-ton bull needs a foot trim, but is reluctant to lie down for the procedure, so Peter has to approach its feet very carefully to avoid his patient falling on him. Julian and Katy operate on a beagle with damaged ligaments, and a cat undergoes a caesarean section - a procedure that is very rarely used on feline patients. The Greens release their calves and donkeys into the field together for the first time, and a tortoise displays a loss of appetite

programme.title: All Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Adaptation of James Herriot's books about life as a vet in the Yorkshire Dales. Fresh out of veterinary college in 1937, James Herriot struggles to find work in his Depression-struck home town of Glasgow. Seeking new opportunities, he pursues a position in the remote village of Darrowby working for eccentric local vet Siegfried Farnon, who is less than impressed by his early efforts. However, a difficult calving gives James a chance to prove his worth. Starring Nicholas Ralph, Samuel West and Diana Rigg

programme.title: We LOVE Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: Celebrity fans including Fern Britton, the Rev Richard Coles, Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen, Patsy Palmer, Sherrie Hewson, Christopher Biggins and Amanda Barrie join in this celebration of David Croft and Jeremy Lloyd's department store-set sitcom, which ran for 70 episodes from 1972 to 1985. Plus, family and friends of Mollie Sugden, John Inman, Trevor Bannister, Frank Thornton and Wendy Richard share their personal memories and never-before-seen private photographs of the cast

programme.title: Funny Women
programme.desc: Friends and collaborators including Ross Noble, Alan Davies, Meera Syal, Morwenna Banks, Jimmy Mulville and Ricky Grover celebrate the former psychiatric nurse's stellar career in comedy. It was the late 1980s when Jo turned to stand-up, braving the male-dominated alternative comedy circuit. Voted Stand Up of the Year at the 1995 British Comedy Awards, her fearless wit made her a darling of the panel and quiz show circuit. The new millennium brought new comedy gravitas as she wrote and starred in nursing series Getting On and Damned, about social workers

programme.desc: Programme exploring the rising number of assaults on police officers, revealing what they face in the line of duty, and the emotional toll these incidents take. Elsewhere in County Durham, PC Caryn Fairburn is on shift when she and her colleague are asked to respond to an argument

programme.desc: Documentary charting the history of the high-security psychiatric hospital from the Victorian era to the present day, examining the diverse treatments and regimes that have been applied during the 150 years it has been in operation

programme.title: Shopping for a New Penis
programme.desc: Documentary following the story of theatre performer Richard from London, whose entire penis was surgically removed due to an aggressive form of penile cancer. Richard is still coming to terms with the physical and emotional impact of this ordeal on his everyday life and relationships, but with a candid sense of humour, he is confronting his situation head-on - sharing his story as a warning to others about a disease on the increase

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis reveals how CCTV and technological advances are helping to ensure convictions, focusing on criminals in London involved in robberies, thefts, drug crimes, street brawls and weapons offences. In Soho, police officers monitor the activity of the area's 100,000 nightly visitors from a CCTV control room in an underground bunker, while in Lambeth the air support unit is called in when an armed gang are seen rampaging through the streets

programme.desc: Daddy teaches Peppa and George a game to play indoors while they wait for the rain to stop

programme.title: Mya Go
programme.desc: Mama Go and Mya Go are on their way to the beach when the car begins to make a strange noise

programme.title: Noddy: Toyland Detective
programme.desc: Noddy has an invitation from Queen Sparklewings to give to Smartysaurus but a big pile of cabbages are blocking the front door

programme.title: Fireman Sam
programme.desc: Pontypandy is holding a contest for its Most Talented Pet. Mandy and Sarah engage in a rivalry over whether Nipper or Lion will win, though Nipper causes trouble when Mandy lets him off his lead

programme.title: Thomas & Friends
programme.desc: In Brazil, Thomas meets Gustavo, a big, blue engine who reminds him very much of Gordon

programme.title: Abby Hatcher
programme.desc: Princess Flug finds a slug named Sparkles and wants her to be her pet. However, Sparkles causes trouble by eating all the leaves in the hotel

programme.title: Paw Patrol
programme.desc: Daring Danny X, dressed as a bird mascot, gets carried away by a couple of eagles who mistake him for one of their own

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Miss Rabbit makes smoothies and gives George a special 'dinosaur' juice to make him strong

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: George needs help to keep hold of his enormous dinosaur balloon

programme.title: Ricky Zoom
programme.desc: DJ cannot play on the new climbing structure because of his extra wheel, so his friends do their best to help out

programme.title: Pirata & Capitano
programme.desc: The crew finds a clue leading to buried treasure, and Meowcat seems to be trying to tell them something

programme.title: Paw Patrol
programme.desc: The residents of Adventure Bay are working hard to win the record for the world's tallest tower of pizzas

programme.title: Floogals
programme.desc: Captain Fleeker, First Officer Flo and Junior Boomer wonder why there is a house in the garden completely made of glass

programme.title: Daisy & Ollie
programme.desc: Daddy surprises Daisy and Ollie with a trip to the cinema, which makes them really excited as they have not been before

programme.title: Shane the Chef
programme.desc: Shane and Izzy help Maggie to pick her strawberries, but a mishap with a football results in the fruits being crushed

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Gerald Giraffe joins Peppa's playgroup and is keen to make friends with everyone, but he finds that being so tall makes playing hide-and-seek quite a challenge

programme.title: Sunny Bunnies
programme.desc: Big Boo tries to play Hopscotch with Iris and Shiny, but finds he is too big and clumsy

programme.title: Jeremy Vine
programme.desc: The broadcaster and guests discuss the issues of the day, with co-host Storm Huntley joining him for phone-ins and reading out viewers' correspondence

programme.title: Can't Pay? We'll Take It Away!
programme.desc: An insight into the world of debt collection from the perspectives of people who owe money, their creditors and those who collect the payments. High court eviction officer Kevin Stokes faces a physical assault, while Paul Bohill and his team deal with a difficult case involving a mother and daughter who are about to be left out on the street with nowhere to go

programme.title: 5 News at Lunchtime
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Dan and Nick tail three suspected drug dealers into a car park, but just as they identify themselves as police officers, the trio speed off. Ant and Mark's dogged pursuit of a stolen moped takes them off their patch into the estates of Sheffield, where they play a game of cat and mouse around the backroads and alleyways

programme.title: Access
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Dean wakes to find Ziggy with a black eye, courtesy of his ex, Amber. Nikau confides in Bella about his connection to the beach

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Dipi prepares to confront Shane about his lies, Kyle feigns illness to garner attention from Sheila, and Toadie allows Karl to weigh in on his romantic dilemma

programme.title: Killer Vacation (2018)
programme.desc: A dream holiday takes a turn for the worse when Jake and his girlfriend Lindsey discover that his ex-wife is staying at the same resort. Thriller, starring Jacob Young

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel spoils Ross's plans of a romantic last evening with Emily before she returns to England by organising a farewell party and inviting everyone. Meanwhile Phoebe's pregnancy causes her to abandon her vegetarian lifestyle as she craves meat. Comedy, guest starring Helen Baxendale

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross pours out his heart to Emily, but is unprepared for her reaction, while Phoebe receives surprising news about her pregnancy. Chandler and Joey get free adult films on their TV and try to make sure they do not disappear

programme.title: 5 News at 5
programme.desc: Round-up of the day's headlines from around the world

programme.title: Neighbours
programme.desc: Dipi prepares to confront Shane about his lies, Kyle feigns illness to garner attention from Sheila, and Toadie allows Karl to weigh in on his romantic dilemma

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Dean wakes to find Ziggy with a black eye, courtesy of his ex, Amber. Nikau confides in Bella about his connection to the beach

programme.title: 5 News Tonight
programme.desc: A round-up of the evening's headlines, including coverage of the latest national and international stories

programme.title: Wild Britain
programme.desc: Cameras follow some of the animals that thrive in inner-city environments across the country. Urban foxes did not exist in Britain until the 1930s, but now these animals inhabit 90% of towns and cities and take residence in built-up living quarters. Meanwhile, cameras observe families of hedgehogs thriving in unkempt gardens, and peregrine falcons nesting in some on London's tallest buildings

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Dr Ajay Singal is visited by Cyril, an 89-year-old who has a mischievous sense of humour and a loving family around him. Yet when it is time to sleep at night, he gets overwhelmed with anxiety and cannot settle. Can his GP get to the bottom of his problem? Paul comes in complaining of an excruciating pain in his throat, diabetic Paula worries about a sudden dip in her blood sugar levels and railway worker Darren shows Dr Qamar some unsightly lumps on his back

programme.title: Elephant Hospital
programme.desc: The work of a Thailand hospital devoted solely to treating elephants. The two-part documentary reveals the day-to-day challenges of dealing with supersized patients suffering everything from deadly viruses to cuts and parasites. It also shows how the facility teaches baby elephants new commands and even turns elephant poo into fertiliser

programme.title: Racism: My Story
programme.desc: One hundred days after the death of George Floyd, a number of black British people share their experiences of racism throughout the decades, including their treatment by police, workplace discrimination or on the streets. Contributors include actor and comedian Andi Osho, barrister and star of The Chase Shaun Wallace, Dawn Butler MP, Dr Janine Channer, football commentator and former player Mark Bright, nurse Neomi Bennett, youth mentor Sayce Holmes-Lewis, and author and journalist Yomi Adegoke

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red
programme.desc: Documentary following emergency response teams in the West Midlands on their most extreme medical callouts, often involving roadside surgery to save people's lives

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. The programme reveals how essential surveillance cameras have become in the fight against crime and features footage of people caught red-handed

programme.title: The 21.co.uk Live Casino Show
programme.desc: Interactive gambling

programme.title: Access
programme.desc: Showbiz news and gossip

programme.title: The Nightmare Neighbour Next Door
programme.desc: A family who have generated more than 600 complaints from angry neighbours move into a quiet suburban street in Blackburn. Plus, a psychiatrist in south London battles a huge renovation next door, while thumping basslines, pounding walls and slamming doors deprive a mother and her family of sleep

programme.title: The Funny Thing About
programme.desc: Johnny Vegas, Anthea Turner, Vanessa Feltz, Carl Fogarty, Nish Kumar and Nina Wadia are joined by a selection of British comedy stars to share their thoughts on the many weird habits, off-putting quirks and wild eccentricities people are willing to put up with while 'in relationships'. Through honest confessionals and witty anecdotes, the assembled talking heads ask how people even know they are officially an item, and celebrate some of the more endearing romantic gestures that come along with the inconveniences of sharing a life with someone else

programme.title: Wildlife SOS
programme.desc: The work of volunteers treating sick, injured and orphaned animals at a wildlife sanctuary

programme.title: Divine Designs
programme.desc: Dr Paul Binski chronicles the development of religious architecture during the 16th and 17th centuries. He contrasts the ornate splendour of the chapel at King's College, Cambridge, with the simplicity of post-Reformation churches

programme.title: House Doctor
programme.desc: Ann Maurice visits a house off a busy road in Wandsworth, south London, where she decides the walls need repainting and the floors require sanding

programme.title: Garfield 2: A Tale of Two Kitties (2006)
programme.desc: The lazy moggy stows away in his owner's luggage and ends up in London. He is promptly mistaken for an English upper-class cat, who has recently inherited a grand estate, and moves into his stately home - much to the dismay of the real aristocratic feline's conniving relation, who is bent on swindling him out of his money. Family comedy, starring Breckin Meyer, Billy Connolly and Jennifer Love Hewitt, with the voices of Bill Murray and Tim Curry

programme.title: The Secret Life of Walter Mitty (2013)
programme.desc: An ordinary man seeks solace from his mundane life by drifting into daydreams where he is a heroic figure. When he and a friend are faced with unemployment, he is propelled into a globetrotting adventure in real life. Comedy drama based on James Thurber's short story, directed by and starring Ben Stiller. With Kristen Wiig, Adam Scott and Shirley MacLaine

programme.title: Rio 2 (2014)
programme.desc: Parrots Jewel and Blu lead a happy life together in Rio with their three chicks, until they find themselves hurled into an expedition up the Amazon. The journey leads to an encounter with an old enemy, while Blu also has to win the approval of Jewel's fearsome father. Animated comedy sequel, with the voices of Jesse Eisenberg, Anne Hathaway and Jamie Foxx

programme.title: Carry On Cabby (1963)
programme.desc: The neglected wife of a workaholic taxi owner grows increasingly tired of his lack of interest in their marriage. In a ploy to bring him to his senses, she secretly launches a rival cab firm with a driver roster made up of attractive women, who soon tempt his customers away. Comedy, starring Sid James, Hattie Jacques, Kenneth Connor and Charles Hawtrey

programme.title: The Monuments Men (2014)
programme.desc: Seven experts in fine art are assembled into a military platoon during the Second World War, and given an unusual mission. They are sent into occupied Europe to recover priceless works of art that have fallen into Nazi hands and ensure the masterpieces survive the conflict. Fact-based drama, directed by and starring George Clooney. With Matt Damon, Bill Murray and Cate Blanchett

programme.title: American Made (2017)
programme.desc: A pilot is recruited to act as a courier for the CIA in the 1980s, carrying out covert deliveries from the US to General Noriega's regime in Panama. However, he exploits the opportunity to set up a profitable sideline trafficking drugs for a cartel. His dangerous double life takes a darker turn when he is ordered to supply weapons to far-right rebel groups operating in Nicaragua. Fact-based comedy drama, starring Tom Cruise and Domhnall Gleeson

programme.title: Get On Up (2014)
programme.desc: Biopic of James Brown, examining key moments in the soul singer's life, from his impoverished childhood in South Carolina to his rise to become one of the most influential figures in music history, and following his intense friendship with fellow singer Bobby Byrd. Starring Chadwick Boseman, Viola Davis, Nelsan Ellis and Octavia Spencer

programme.title: Trance (2013)
programme.desc: An art dealer becomes involved in a plot to steal a valuable painting from his own auction house, but a blow to the head causes him to develop amnesia. Unable to remember the stolen item's whereabouts, he starts seeing a hypnotist to recall where he left it, but as she searches the depths of his mind, the barriers between fiction and reality begin to blur. Danny Boyle's non-linear crime thriller, starring James McAvoy and Rosario Dawson

programme.title: The Rugrats Movie (1998)
programme.desc: Tommy tries to return his new brother to the baby shop, helped by Angelica, Chuckie, Phil and Lil - but the friends become lost in dense woodlands en route, turning their expedition into a scary and exciting journey of discovery. Animated comedy based on the TV series, with the voices of Elizabeth Daily, Christine Cavanaugh, Kath Soucie, Whoopi Goldberg and Busta Rhymes

programme.title: Time Bandits (1981)
programme.desc: A bemused schoolboy is escorted on a bizarre journey through time by a group of larcenous dwarfs. His adventures lead him to encounters with various characters from history - but none of them are quite what he had imagined. Terry Gilliam's fantasy comedy, starring Craig Warnock, David Rappaport, John Cleese, Michael Palin, Sean Connery, Ian Holm, Ralph Richardson and David Warner

programme.title: Baggage Claim (2013)
programme.desc: An air hostess is horrified at the thought of her younger sister getting married while she is still single. She realises her job gives her access to eligible men from across America, and begins clocking up thousands of air miles as she scours the country for a potential fiance. Romantic comedy, with Paula Patton and Taye Diggs

programme.title: The Choice (2016)
programme.desc: Two neighbours find themselves overwhelmingly attracted to each other, and make substantial changes in their lives to be together - one breaking off a long-term relationship and the other conquering his fear of commitment. Over the next 10 years, their feelings for each other are tested by a series of challenges. Romantic drama based on Nicholas Sparks' novel, starring Benjamin Walker and Teresa Palmer

programme.title: The Way Way Back (2013)
programme.desc: An introverted teenager joins his mother and her overbearing boyfriend on an awkward family holiday. He befriends a free-spirited employee at the nearby water park, and the relationship inspires him to assert his independence for the first time. Comedy drama, starring Liam James, Sam Rockwell, Steve Carell, Toni Collette and Allison Janney

programme.title: Die Hard 4.0 (2007)
programme.desc: Tough New York cop John McClane is sent to escort a computer hacker to Washington for questioning. During this routine operation he stumbles on a criminal mastermind's plan to launch a technological terrorist attack on the US, and is forced to enlist the hacker's aid in foiling the evil scheme. Action thriller sequel, with Bruce Willis, Timothy Olyphant, Justin Long and Maggie Q

programme.title: Everly (2014)
programme.desc: A woman forced into prostitution by a criminal overlord begins working with the police in an attempt to bring him down. He decides to silence her forever, leading to a bloody confrontation between her and his henchmen which she must win to stand any chance of saving her life, as well as her daughter and mother. Action thriller, starring Salma Hayek, Akie Kotabe and Laura Cepeda

programme.title: Drowning by Numbers (1988)
programme.desc: Cissie Colpitts drowns her husband after becoming sick of his philandering ways, and enlists the aid of local coroner Madgett to cover up her crime. However, when the spouses of Cissie's identically named daughter and granddaughter meet similar fates, the unfortunate Madgett is cajoled into hiding yet more murders. Black comedy, starring Joan Plowright, Juliet Stevenson, Joely Richardson and Bernard Hill

programme.title: Sword of Sherwood Forest (1960)
programme.desc: Legendary outlaw hero Robin Hood rallies his merry men to thwart a dastardly plan by the Sheriff of Nottingham to murder the Archbishop of Canterbury, who has denounced the sheriff's plot to confiscate another nobleman's land. Medieval adventure, starring Richard Greene, Peter Cushing, Niall MacGinnis, Richard Pasco, Sarah Branch and Oliver Reed

programme.title: The Man in the Iron Mask (1977)
programme.desc: The despotic king of France imprisons his identical twin and sees to it that he always wears a metal mask to avoid being identified, but musketeer D'Artagnan has a plan to restore the rightful heir to the throne. Swashbuckling adventure, based on the classic story by Alexandre Dumas, starring Richard Chamberlain in a dual role, with Patrick McGoohan, Louis Jourdan and Jenny Agutter

programme.title: Faintheart (2008)
programme.desc: Family man Richard finds solace from his humdrum life in the world of historical battle re-enactments. However, when his hobby sees him spend more time on the battlefield than at home, his wife leaves him for a gym instructor, forcing Richard to reassess his priorities. Romantic comedy, starring Eddie Marsan, Ewen Bremner, Jessica Hynes, Bronagh Gallagher, Tim Healy and Paul Nicholls

programme.title: Big Stone Gap (2014)
programme.desc: A self-proclaimed spinster finds her life shaken up after learning a long-buried family secret. Romantic comedy, starring Ashley Judd and Patrick Wilson

programme.title: Footloose (2011)
programme.desc: A teenager from Boston moves to a small conservative town, and discovers the council and a preacher have enforced a ban on loud music and dancing. He openly defies the harsh rules, revitalising the local youth and winning the heart of the reverend's daughter. Remake of the 1984 drama, with Kenny Wormald, Julianne Hough and Dennis Quaid

programme.title: American Ultra (2015)
programme.desc: Small-town store clerk and unmotivated stoner Mike is enjoying his life with girlfriend Phoebe when his world is suddenly turned upside down. He is unaware he is actually a highly trained, lethal government sleeper agent who has been targeted for assassination, forcing him to summon his inner action-hero in order to survive. Action comedy, starring Jesse Eisenberg and Kristen Stewart

programme.title: 47 Metres Down (2017)
programme.desc: Two sisters vacationing in Mexico are trapped in a shark cage at the bottom of the ocean. With less than an hour of oxygen left and great white sharks circling nearby, they must fight to survive. Horror, starring Mandy Moore, Claire Holt and Matthew Modine

programme.title: Pili (2017)
programme.desc: A Tanzanian woman with HIV works the land in a backbreaking job for a heartbreakingly low wage in order to feed her two children. But then an opportunity arises that could change Pili's life for the better. Director Leanne Welham's drama, starring Bello Rashid. In Swahili with English subtitles

programme.title: Lovelace (2013)
programme.desc: Biopic of 1970s adult film star Linda Lovelace, contrasting her glamorous reputation following the release of groundbreaking adult movie Deep Throat - which made her an international star - to the dark account of exploitation and abuse she revealed to the world years later. Starring Amanda Seyfried, Peter Sarsgaard, Sharon Stone and Robert Patrick

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Lainey, Erica and Barry plan a big Risky Business-inspired party at Lainey's house while her father is out of town. However, when the gathering gets out of control, Beverly has to come to their rescue. Elsewhere, Adam annoys Murray by talking to Dana on the phone for hours on end

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly tries to help Adam navigate a painless path through puberty, but just as his voice is changing, he is cast in the lead role of the school musical. Determined to help him survive the performance, Beverly decides lip-synching might be the answer. Meanwhile, Murray is irked when Erica chooses musician Johnny over Barry's sidekick Geoff as her date for the homecoming dance

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples on the Sussex Coast compete for the cash prize of £1,000, serving up a divisive cold soup, home comfort food, and a pescatarian feast with a Salmon Wellington

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: The end is nigh for fantasy fan Evan as he plans to wage war on his wedding day, but his emotional bride-to-be Zoe wants a low-key family affair

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The couples reunite for a dinner party, the first time they have come together since Dean and Davina's affair was revealed at the previous week's fiery commitment ceremony

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Lennox starts an internship at Mel's office and immediately hits it off with someone who happens to work for her biggest political rival, which leads to an accusation of disloyalty. Elsewhere, Joe finally finds a new job in the financial industry, which entails working overnight as an online consultant for a Russian company

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: When Jake's favourite pizzeria burns down, the fire marshal (Patton Oswalt) believes the owner should be the prime suspect in the investigation. Outraged, the detective does everything in his power to prove him wrong, even if it means stepping outside his jurisdiction. Meanwhile, chaos ensues at the precinct after a computer virus publishes everyone's internet browser history

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Santiago hosts a Thanksgiving dinner at her apartment for all her colleagues, but the event risks turning into a disaster, especially when Holt and Peralta have to leave in a hurry to catch a thief who has stolen a large sum of money from the evidence lock-up. Can Boyle save the day?

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: The eccentric youngster takes it upon himself to find the perfect neighbours to buy the house next door. Missy is troubled when she has to pitch against her boyfriend

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: The eccentric youngster makes a scientific breakthrough at the dentist's office. Dale and Meemaw go to the casino and Georgie makes a big mistake when he is left in charge of the store

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Amy and Sheldon start a gossip experiment to see how quickly rumours spread among the gang, while Howard tells his friends he is planning to propose to Bernadette - not realising Raj is wrestling with his own feelings for her. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Mayim Bialik and Johnny Galecki

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon is infuriated when Priya uses her legal knowledge to find a loophole in the room-mate agreement - but his spirits are lifted by a night on the town with Amy, Penny and Bernadette. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons, Johnny Galecki and Kaley Cuoco

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Adam and Andi's babysitter cancels on Valentine's Day so they leave Kate in charge for the first time. Don forgets to make romantic plans for Marcy so he steals Adam's ideas

programme.title: Man with a Plan
programme.desc: Adam struggles to curb his overprotective ways as Kate prepares to go out on her first date. Meanwhile, Don enlists Lowell to help him find the perfect anniversary gift for Marcy

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam and Barry team up to form a rap super-group just like the Beastie Boys. Beverly puts pressure on Murray to let her redesign the kitchen, but when he refuses to allow it she comes up with a devious plan

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: In an attempt to get closer to Barry and Erica, Beverly promises to bring actor Scott Baio to town for a big charity event. Adam gets into a tricky situation when he arranges to take both his best friend Emmy and his girlfriend Dana to the school dance

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta tries to set a new record on a training course, Diaz does not receive an invitation to Boyle's wedding, and Holt becomes addicted to a game application on his smartphone. Comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Boyle asks Peralta to be his best man, but as well as helping out with cake-tasting and choosing invitations, he must also persuade the groom to start standing up to Vivian before the big day. Holt helps Diaz make a sincere apology and Jeffords, Santiago and Gina embark on a tough new diet - but struggle to stick to it

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: In an attempt to bond with Sheldon, George Sr takes the boys on a road trip to see the Space Shuttle launch. Meanwhile, Mary, Missy and Meemaw have a girls' weekend at the salon. Narrated by Jim Parsons

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: The young genius is forced to tutor Georgie when poor grades threaten his brother's future on the football team. In doing so, he manages to pick up a few skills himself. US comedy, starring Iain Armitage and Montana Jordan

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Sheldon's mother comes to visit and woos the gang, but he cannot help feeling jealous of all the attention she is receiving - until a bout of illness lays him low, and he suddenly sees the benefits of having her around. Comedy, starring Jim Parsons

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Leonard has doubts about his feelings for Priya when he kisses a girl he meets in the comic-book store, while the rest of the friends play pranks on Sheldon in celebration of Halloween. Comedy, starring Johnny Galecki and Jim Parsons

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: The day of Kyle's funeral arrives, and Brooke worries about the words she has written for Kyle. Meanwhile, Warren breaks into Toby and Celeste's flat to surprise Felix

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The events of last night's dinner party spill over into the third commitment ceremony, as many of the couples are suffering from a lack of affection and intimacy

programme.title: Jack Ryan: Shadow Recruit (2014)
programme.desc: A former soldier injured in the line of duty takes up a new career as a financial analyst for the CIA. However, his deskbound position changes to a dangerous undercover assignment when he discovers a plot to destroy the US economy. Spy thriller inspired by Tom Clancy's series of novels, starring Chris Pine, Keira Knightley, Kevin Costner and Kenneth Branagh, who also directed

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
programme.desc: Part one of three. Buffy decides it is not safe to stay in Sunnydale now Glory knows her sister is the Key, so she and the gang flee for their lives. However, they are attacked on the road by horsemen from the Knights of Byzantium who have been sent to kill Dawn. The slayer learns what Glory intends to do with the Key, and calls Ben in to treat an injured Giles - unaware of the danger his presence puts them all in

programme.title: Buffy the Vampire Slayer
programme.desc: Part two of three. Willow uses magic to enter a catatonic Buffy's mind and return her to consciousness. Dawn tries to persuade Ben to help her escape as Glory's two personalities become increasingly confused, while Spike and Xander visit Doc as they seek a means to kill the Hellgod. Guest starring Joel Grey

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: Howard questions his engineering abilities when he and Sheldon can't get a toy drone to fly. A cancelled flight almost stops Leonard from giving the commencement address at his former high school, while Raj pits his parents against each other when his father cuts him off financially

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory
programme.desc: There is a clash of personalities when Sheldon and Leonard's mothers finally meet, while Howard finally decides to do his fair share of the housework - but not without a little help from his friends. Guest starring Christine Baranski and Laurie Metcalf

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's armchair critics share more of their critiques of the week's best shows, including The Crown, Question Time Leaders Special, Seven Worlds, One Planet, I'm a Celebrity, Good Morning Britain, Dom Does America, A Very Yorkshire Brothel, and Buy It Now for Christmas

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Modern-day lothario Lewis, 27, arrives at the restaurant with a tried-and-tested plan to charm his way into the affections of 23-year-old flight attendant Megan, while 24-year-old Lee has to hold his own against the sharp humour of 29-year-old Marky. Interactive documentary in which singletons meet up with possible partners from the internet for the first time

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: The end is nigh for fantasy fan Evan as he plans to wage war on his wedding day, but his emotional bride-to-be Zoe wants a low-key family affair

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples on the Sussex Coast compete for the cash prize of £1,000, serving up a divisive cold soup, home comfort food, and a pescatarian feast with a Salmon Wellington

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Barry accidentally records over one of Beverly's prized family videotapes - her treasured keepsake from his fifth birthday party. Meanwhile, Adam and Murray build a robot together, but the project comes between them when they experience creative differences

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam goes to great lengths to dodge swimming classes, but his plan to avoid the sport could affect his friendship with Dave. Jealousy prompts Barry to undermine Erica when she attempts to make the world a better place by forming a school social awareness club

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Three couples from Hampshire compete, beginning with an evening of Eastern European cuisine with Stevan and Clare, followed by Josh and Bettie, and James and Victoria

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Rugby-loving groom-to-be Wes plans a wedding in front of 26,000 people at a Premiership match, but his fiancee Charlotte is dreaming of a much more intimate day

programme.title: Married at First Sight Australia
programme.desc: The events of last night's dinner party spill over into the third commitment ceremony, as many of the couples are suffering from a lack of affection and intimacy

programme.title: Melissa & Joey
programme.desc: Mel is annoyed when Joe's Russian girlfriend Elena comes to visit and begins to monopolise his time. Meanwhile, Ryder volunteers to help Lennox with an experiment that requires him to eat only vending machine food, but he soon comes to regret his generous offer

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta is put in charge of Captain Holt's security after he receives several death threats - a responsibility he not only enjoys, but also abuses as much as he can. Santiago tries to encourage her colleagues to take a Christmas photo, only to be met with a lack of enthusiasm - and Diaz's refusal to smile. US cop comedy, starring Andy Samberg

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Peralta tries to make a deal with a suspect Diaz has just arrested in an attempt to catch a car thief he has been tracking for years. Boyle's colleagues set out to meet his every need when he returns to work after being shot in the line of duty, and Captain Holt struggles to find a home for two puppies

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: The eccentric youngster discovers that Mary has been keeping a secret about his future. Meemaw is furious when she learns Dale fired Georgie

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.desc: Pilot episode of the comedy prequel to The Big Bang Theory about nine-year-old Sheldon Cooper. He possesses a once-in-a-generation mind capable of advanced mathematics and science, but finds that gift isn't always helpful growing up in East Texas, a land where church and football are king. Iain Armitage stars

programme.title: The Big Bang Theory

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Josh and Rhian Cooper, who plan to build a stylish home on the cliffs overlooking the seaside town of Aberdovey, on the coast of Gwynedd. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners of the African-themed Foxholm B&B in North Yorkshire begins another competition, trying to prove their establishment offers the best value for money. programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Wendy Johnson and John Shields welcome their rival hoteliers to the Pack Horse, an American diner-themed B&B in Lincolnshire, for the second round of the competition. A horse-riding session turns sour when Steve Reed takes a dislike to Nick Lawler, and matters get worse over dinner - before a pancake shortage at breakfast causes problems for the hosts

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Steve and Sigrid Reed host at their Glenwalden B&B in Blackpool. Nick Lawler seems to be warming to the owners, but one of them suspects his change of heart is all part of a cunning plan

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Nick Lawler and business partner Kate Wilson host the competition at the Winder Hall Country House B&B in the Lake District, but a clash of egos ensues when they try to lecture the other participants about how to manage their hospitality establishments, and a disastrous breakfast service leaves contestants unimpressed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Spider monkey Kiara becomes a mother for the first time, but keepers worry that the baby is becoming weak as Kiara appears to be more interested in playing with the other monkeys than taking on maternal responsibilities. Meanwhile, baby capybara Henry enjoys having the attention of all the female capybaras, and Babirusa Sausu moves from paddock to paddock to mate with various females

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Emma and Nigel Forde and their three boys have lived in their Essex house for the past seven years, but after a bad experience with a bathroom fitting they have let the maintenance of the family home slide, which means broken kitchen doors and some rather unusual curtains. Nigel thinks it's time to wave the white flag and sell up, but Emma wants some real investment to turn around the house she loves. Kirstie Allsopp has plenty of refurbishment ideas, while Phil Spencer has already found some viable replacement homes to buy. Will the Fordes decide to stay or go?

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick visits Stephanie to help get the original chateau pizza oven working, while Annabelle and Justin return from holiday to find their chateau infested with bees

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Colin Mackinnon and his partner Marta Briongos take on an ambitious project to build a bespoke metal home next to an airfield runway, designed by one of Scotland's most eminent architects and inspired by aircraft hangars. They have to contend with violent rain, snow, 100mph winds and the worst storms for a century, and the couple have to draw on their reserves of mental energy, as what was supposed to be a 12-month build heads into a third year

programme.title: Life After WWI: In Colour
programme.desc: Part one of two. Historical documentary featuring colourised archive footage to show what life was like in Europe after the end of the First World War. The defeat of the vast German, Austro-Hungarian and Ottoman empires rekindled hope among many regional and ethnic groups who had dreamt of independence for centuries, but in central Europe civil wars and sporadic conflicts erupted

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Medics battle to keep 48-year-old Paul alive as he suffers a series of heart attacks. Roger's emergency stop halts his day at a scooter rally while, in Cumbria, there's a dramatic mountainside rescue for seriously injured cyclist Nick

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Josh and Rhian Cooper, who plan to build a stylish home on the cliffs overlooking the seaside town of Aberdovey, on the coast of Gwynedd. programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits three previous restaurants he tried to save, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or turned back into nightmares. First he heads for West Babylon, New York, where he helped the inexperienced owners of restaurant Classic American deal with their escalating debts, before returning to Davide in Boston, an Italian restaurant run by two warring brothers. Finally, he visits Down City, a restaurant in Providence, Rhode Island, run by hot-headed owner Abby

programme.title: Food Unwrapped

Meanwhile, Jimmy Doherty heads to Ireland to investigate whether traditional rolled oats are healthier than quick-cook porridge

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Sara Damergi takes Savita and Vinod Patel from Hertfordshire house-hunting in and around the picturesque town of Denia on Spain's Costa Blanca. The couple must choose between the convenience of the town and the amazing views on offer just a little farther afield

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick visits Stephanie to help get the original chateau pizza oven working, while Annabelle and Justin return from holiday to find their chateau infested with bees

programme.title: Building the Dream

The presenter reveals how a few simple changes to the blueprints could save the couple thousands of pounds, and they visit a self-built property in the Scottish Borders for inspiration and motivation

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners of the Sea Verge Hotel in Paignton, Devon, try to prove their B&B offers the best value for money. However, Tony and Chris Fulbrook's guests are far from happy, with Christine Hale complaining about the noise from the sea and scolding Chris for serving breakfast in her slippers

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition moves on to the Portcullis Hotel in Chipping Sodbury, Gloucestershire, where mother-and-daughter owners Christine and Jenna Hale make a poor first impression by cajoling their guests into taking part in a karaoke session that runs until 2am. Matters worsen the following day, as a caricaturing workshop does little to alleviate the tension among the contestants

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Entrepreneur Andy Scott hosts at the Orchid Hotel in Bournemouth, and takes on Richard Bailey in the boxing gym. Christine and Jenna Hale are unhappy to find foreign objects in their beds, and a spot of flirting is brought to a swift halt when the owner receives some negative feedback

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition heads to Orles Barn in Ross-on-Wye, Herefordshire, where owners Richard and Kelly Bailey impress their guests with the beauty and quality of the accommodation. The contestants then head to a local organic supplier to pick asparagus for their dinner

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, keepers prepare for the difficult task of transporting giraffe Kidepo to a new zoo, and Kidepo's brother Narus is not making the move any easier. Meanwhile, silvery gibbon Alvin is keen to get close to his partner Tilu again, and after diving headfirst into an empty pool one too many times, keepers decide a trip to the vet is in order for caiman lizard Gary to see if there is an underlying problem. Elsewhere, dominant warthog Magnum is being challenged by fellow warthog Dobby, so keepers decide it is time to move Dobby to a zoo in Germany

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: In Bradford, West Yorkshire, Michelle is tired of the abode she shares with Andrew. The bungalow, set in rolling hills, is small and was only supposed to be a temporary home - that was a decade ago. However, Andrew loves this place. Kirstie tries to convince Michelle to stay by solving her kitchen problem and adding some feminine touches to the decor. Phil, meanwhile, has found a selection of amazing properties to buy - including a bungalow that's twice the size and a barn conversion with lots of outside space

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Angel goes shopping with Stephanie and shows her the best way to frame her father's original artwork using modern and antique fabric. Justin and Annabelle put up their newly renovated exterior chateau shutters, but the numbering seems to have gone a bit haywire, so getting the shutters on the right windows proves a challenge

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Tamayo Hussey, who has missed Japan since she moved to the UK 14 years earlier with her husband, quantum physics professor Nigel. The couple want to transform a 1960s forester's lodge into a unique Japanese house, complete with a tatami room - decorated in her homeland's traditional style - sliding paper walls and a bath on the roof. Keen to keep costs down, they have decided to engage only the design skills - rather than the full services - of an architect, so with no previous building experience they tackle the project alone

programme.title: Double Jeopardy (1999)
programme.desc: A man fakes his own death and frames his wife for murder, but after six years in prison she is released. Since the woman has already served her time, she has nothing to lose by really killing him - but her mission of vengeance is complicated by a zealous parole officer determined to stop her. Thriller, starring Ashley Judd, Tommy Lee Jones, Bruce Greenwood and Annabeth Gish

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Doctors run tests on 89-year-old Alyce, who has been brought to King's by ambulance after falling near her home. She's accompanied by her close friend Linda, and reveals her resolve to make sure she lives long enough to celebrate her next birthday. Soon after, 23-year-old groom-to-be Sean is examined following an epileptic seizure lasting most of the night

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts, with Sean Lock and Stephen Mangan taking on guest captain Richard Ayoade and Katherine Ryan. Bill Bailey is in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley is in charge of the letters and numbers

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Medics fly to the aid of mountain biker Rob in the Cumbrian mountains. Off-road motorbiker Dean is set to miss his friend's 50th birthday celebrations after taking a nasty tumble. Plus, 15-year-old Robbie needs urgent care when he falls from a coastal cliff in South Shields

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Pete and Jen Chumley, who hope to leave their current home in the suburbs of Edinburgh and build their own five-bedroom family house in rural Perthshire. The presenter reveals how a few simple changes to the blueprints could save the couple thousands of pounds, and they visit a self-built property in the Scottish Borders for inspiration and motivation

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Jimmy Doherty investigates supermarkets' stringent criteria for the perfect vegetable, and the incredible lengths to which farmers go to grow produce that looks more attractive. In America, Kate Quiton examines why wine is getting stronger and learns about a machine that has been removing excess alcohol from some Californian wines since the 1990s. Matt Tebbutt takes on the ultimate tea-break challenge as he tries to settle an age-old dispute once and for all: which biscuit is best for dunking?

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance for viewers to purchase goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: In this bite-sized episode, Kirstie Allsopp plans to surprise her guests with two main courses - roast beef with mulled wine gravy, and masala cauliflower with coconut stuffing for the vegetarians

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Summer Sun
programme.desc: Peter and Karen Greaves from Wiltshire want a bolthole on Spain's Costa Blanca that they can escape to at a moment's notice. Sara Damergi shows them a selection of properties for £50,000 or less

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Max McMurdo restore and upcycle 2CV headlamps, a children's rocking horse, a Victorian streetlamp and a high chair

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien explore a fairground, and collect a bicycle made for two, a ship's search lamp and a lambing chair

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Angel goes shopping with Stephanie and shows her the best way to frame her father's original artwork using modern and antique fabric. Justin and Annabelle put up their newly renovated exterior chateau shutters, but the numbering seems to have gone a bit haywire, so getting the shutters on the right windows proves a challenge

programme.title: Building the Dream
programme.desc: Charlie Luxton meets Sally and John Lane, who plan to build an energy-efficient kit home in Over, Cambridgeshire, that will be made in Germany and then shipped to their plot for assembly. The couple also visit a similar-style property in Hertfordshire for ideas and inspiration

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners of the musically themed Harmony B&B in Torquay, Devon, begin the competition, trying to prove their establishment offers the best value for money. Dutch hosts Hans MacDaniel and Monica van der Meij try to impress their guests, but it remains to be seen what they will make of wheelchair badminton, saxophones at breakfast and a controversial sausage

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest continues at Chimneys B&B in North Walsham, Norfolk, where owners Jenny and Richard Harmer's decision to use a flag as a tablecloth angers Hans MacDaniel and Monica Van Der Meij. The argument seems to abate during an afternoon canoeing session - only to resurface at breakfast the following day

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Beverley Conoley and Caroline Pocklington host at the Wordsworth Hotel in Cockermouth, Cumbria. They take their guests to a spa and Hans MacDaniel happily volunteers for a spray tan, while Richard Harmer is far more uncomfortable as he finds himself on a massage table. The day ends with dinner at a nearby bistro, but Jenny Harmer complains about the temperature

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Mother and daughter Lesley and Emily Gray play host at the Park Farm Guest House in Gateshead, but while the evening's entertainment featuring a ride on a two-wheeled personal transporter goes down well among the guests, tensions simmer when the dinner conversation turns to a delicate issue

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants meet one last time to learn how much they have been paid by their fellow hoteliers before discovering who has won the week's competition. Old tensions resurface as the results are announced, and there are heated exchanges around the table

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Thirty seven-year-old elephant Thi is pregnant for the ninth time but the keepers believe she is now three months' overdue. The risks for Thi and her unborn calf are growing by the day, and the staff are worried. Rampant male Rothschild giraffe Meru causes problems for the giraffe herd as he relentlessly chases the females, and male clownfish Polo gets injured while fighting with the other males for the attentions of dominant female Rosie. However, clownfish can change sex at will, and Polo's time alone in an isolation tank produces a surprising side effect

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer come to the aid of the Oberoi family from Harrow, whose house has a fridge freezer and in the hallway and a single room for their two children. Kirstie comes up with a plan to expand the kitchen and make the lounge more welcoming, while Phil shows them some of the other homes on the market

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Dick visits Stephanie to check out the new boiler for her mum's apartment - but it's bad news and mum Isobel is not happy. Justin and Annabelle must get busy to complete rooms in time for a visit from Justin's 95-year-old grandmother. Jayne and Steve battle with a giant four poster bed for the master en suite bedroom, but with a little help from some friends, manage to complete another room

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Ben and Rachel Hammond have spent a year searching London for somewhere exciting to live with their two daughters, and they have finally stumbled on a 1950s home on a unique plot, situated within the leafy depths of a park in the south of the city. The only problem is the house itself - an ugly, inefficient and uninspiring property - and their solution is to radically transform it into a sleek, modernist masterpiece. But this is no easy task, as Kevin McCloud finds out

programme.title: George Clarke's Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: The architect meets a Yorkshire couple who plan to convert a German fire engine into a cabin on wheels and go on a road trip across Europe. George also visits a fly-tipping site being transformed into a nature reserve with a community classroom, while the next stage of the Spanish tour features a cutting edge cantilever house

programme.title: Kiri
programme.desc: Mystery drama, starring Sarah Lancashire. Social worker Miriam is caught up in a police investigation when a young girl called Kiri goes missing on one of her supervised visits. As the search for her intensifies, Miriam is publicly blamed by the police, the press, and even her colleagues for putting the girl at risk

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Among those arriving at King's College Hospital is 56-year-old Malcolm, who is put on life support after a cardiac arrest. Nightclub host Phil, 36, receives treatment for chronic pain in his back and side, and realises his party lifestyle is taking its toll, while medics discover 61-year-old Bob has suffered a stroke. Doctors also see to Kamilla, 12, who has been knocked down by a car on a pedestrian crossing

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the comedy panel show's version of the words-and-numbers quiz, with Jon Richardson and Cariad Lloyd taking on guest captain Alan Carr and Kevin Bridges. Elis James and John Robins join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: The first episode of the hit comedy. The Craggy Island cleric dreams of achieving stardom on the Faith of Our Fathers TV show - but his hopes are dashed by Jack and Dougal's untimely intervention. Dermot Morgan, Ardal O'Hanlon and Frank Kelly star

programme.title: Father Ted
programme.desc: A sense of dread descends on Craggy Island as the priests prepare for poker-faced Father Stone's annual visit - and Ted tries desperately to think of a scheme to get rid of him. Clerical comedy, starring Dermot Morgan

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay revisits four restaurants he earlier tried to save, finding out whether they stayed on the road to recovery or fell by the wayside. First he visits the Spanish Pavillion in New Jersey, then heads to California to see the owners of Kingston Cafe, French-themed La Frite, and Capri

programme.title: Father Ted
