programme.title: Untamed Romania
programme.desc: In Romania, ancient forests and expansive wetlands provide habitats to some of Europe's most iconic creatures. This is a celebration of nature, in all its beauty and diversity

programme.title: Jamie's School Dinners
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver takes over the kitchen of Kidbrooke School in Greenwich, hoping to re-educate the junk food-loving children by introducing a healthy diet to improve concentration and eliminate long-term problems of obesity and heart disease. He joins forces with dinner lady Nora in his Feed Me Better campaign, aiming to promote nutritious dishes throughout the capital's schools before encouraging the rest of the country to follow suit

programme.title: Saturday Night Out (1963)
programme.desc: Five sailors spend their overnight leave in London, where they get involved with a variety of unusual characters. Drama, starring Bernard Lee, Heather Sears and John Bonney

programme.title: Ill Met By Moonlight (1957)
programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War thriller following the exploits of a squadron of British officers who head into Nazi-occupied Crete to kidnap a German general from under his comrades' noses, but struggle to transport their prisoner back to Cairo unnoticed. Starring Dirk Bogarde, Marius Goring and Cyril Cusack. Directed by frequent collaborators Michael Powell and Emeric Pressburger

programme.desc: A look into Shakespeare's world and the myths surrounding his works

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Dallas Campbell examines films from the Horizon archive to discover what science can teach people about dogs. He investigates the origins of domestic breeds and learns about the role humans played in shaping canine evolution, before asking why the bond between mankind and dogs is particularly strong - and whether it can be improved in the future

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Dr Giles Yeo investigates the latest diet craze - 'clean eating', the social media sensation that suggests certain foods we eat can actually cleanse our bodies and make us healthier. First, Giles meets with an Instagram entrepreneur behind one of the most popular brands associated with clean eating, before travelling to America to learn more about highly influential alternative health advocates

programme.desc: Three friends stumble across £100,000, and spend it all on a shopping spree and a wild party. However, thwey then discover that the money belongs to a feared gangster - and he expects them to pay back every penny. Comedy, starring Joivan Wade, Percelle Ascott and Dee Kaate

programme.title: The Conversations (2016)
programme.desc: A disillusioned dentist tries to become a stand-up comic, but finds he is unable to perform on stage due to his unrequited love for an American woman who is about to return to the States, never to be seen again. In desperation he seeks a way to have her permanently erased from his memory. Comedy drama, starring Haider Zafar and Daniella Down

programme.title: Queens
programme.desc: 16th-century Europe was a hotbed of tense international intrigue. Mary Stuart and Elizabeth I confront one another on matters religious, political and personal

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: John and Patsy Emmerson hope to buy a dream holiday home in Gran Canaria for £100,000. They check out a beachside apartment, a country house, a hillside villa and a luxury retreat. Presented by Amanda Lamb

programme.title: A Place by the Sea
programme.desc: A Glasgow family searching for a holiday home in North Devon are shown around six properties, including a fisherman's cottage in the picturesque village of Appledore, a modern apartment overlooking Woolacombe Beach and an old grange in Lee Bay

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Vernon Kay presents a festive battle between two of ITV1's biggest soap families, hoping to win up to £30,000 for a charity of their choice. Coronation Street's Michael Le Vell (Kevin Webster) teams up with screen wife Sally Whittaker (Sally), Helen Flanagan and Brooke Vincent (who play daughters Rosie and Sophie) and Peter Armitage (Kevin's dad Bill) to play for Genesis Breast Cancer. They face Emmerdale's King family, with Nick Miles (Jimmy King) joined by Nicola Wheeler (partner Nicola), Tom Lister (brother Carl), Kelsey-Beth Crossley (half-sister Scarlett) and Lucy Pargeter (Carl's on-off lover Chas Dingle). Their charities are St Anne's Community Services and Safe at Last

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Former athlete Colin Jackson and broadcaster Esther Rantzen are joined by their families as they compete to win up to £30,000 for their chosen causes, Cardiff-based youth charity Fairbridge and ChildLine respectively. Hosted by Vernon Kay

programme.desc: Brentford v Yeovil Town. Action from the 2012/13 League One play-off final at Wembley Stadium, where the remaining place in the following season's Championship was decided.
